    57	class ParseService:
    58	    """Core PDF parsing, cleaning and chunking utilities.
    59	
    60	    This class is intentionally stateless across calls, except that some helper
    61	    methods compute global signals (e.g., header/footer texts) from a list of
    62	    pages to support per-page cleaning heuristics.
    63	    """
    64	
    65	    HEADER_FOOTER_REGION_RATIO = 0.10  # top/bottom 10%
    66	    HEADER_FOOTER_FREQ_THRESHOLD = 0.60  # appears in >60% pages
    67	
    68	    TARGET_MIN_TOKENS = 150
    69	    TARGET_MAX_TOKENS = 300
    70	    OVERLAP_TOKENS = 50
    71	
    72	    SENTENCE_DELIMS = "。！？；.!?"  # Chinese + English basic punctuation
    73	
    74	    # -------------------------- Public API --------------------------
    75	    def extract_pages(self, pdf_bytes: bytes) -> List[PageInfo]:
    76	        """Use PyMuPDF to extract all pages with text blocks and geometries.
    77	
    78	        Returns a list of PageInfo entries, one per page.
    79	        """
    80	        doc = fitz.open(stream=pdf_bytes, filetype="pdf")
    81	        try:
    82	            pages: List[PageInfo] = []
    83	            for pi, page in enumerate(doc, start=1):
    84	                rect = page.rect
    85	                rotation = int(page.rotation or 0)
    86	                width_pt = float(rect.width)
    87	                height_pt = float(rect.height)
    88	
    89	                # Extract text as dictionary to access spans and sizes
    90	                page_dict = page.get_text("dict")
    91	                blocks: List[BlockInfo] = []
    92	                for blk in page_dict.get("blocks", []):
    93	                    if blk.get("type", 0) != 0:  # 0=text, 1=image, etc.
    94	                        continue
    95	                    lines = blk.get("lines", [])
    96	                    if not lines:
    97	                        continue
    98	
    99	                    # Emit one BlockInfo per line for precise bbox granularity
   100	                    for line_info in self._extract_line_blocks(pi, lines):
   101	                        blocks.append(line_info)
   102	
   103	                pages.append(
   104	                    PageInfo(
   105	                        page_number=pi,
   106	                        width_pt=width_pt,
   107	                        height_pt=height_pt,
   108	                        rotation=rotation,
   109	                        blocks=blocks,
   110	                    )
   111	                )
   112	
   113	            return pages
   114	        finally:
   115	            # Always close the PDF document to free resources
   116	            doc.close()
   117	
   118	    def extract_pages_ocr(
   119	        self, pdf_bytes: bytes, languages: str = "eng+chi_sim", dpi: int = 300
   120	    ) -> List[PageInfo]:
   121	        """Extract pages using Tesseract OCR via PyMuPDF.
   122	
   123	        Same interface as extract_pages() but uses OCR for scanned PDFs.
   124	        Requires Tesseract to be installed on the system.
   125	        """
   126	        import logging
   127	        logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
   128	        doc = fitz.open(stream=pdf_bytes, filetype="pdf")
   129	        try:
   130	            pages: List[PageInfo] = []
   131	            for pi, page in enumerate(doc, start=1):
   132	                try:
   133	                    rect = page.rect
   134	                    rotation = int(page.rotation or 0)
   135	                    width_pt = float(rect.width)
   136	                    height_pt = float(rect.height)
   137	
   138	                    # Cap rendered image size at 20MP to prevent Tesseract crashes
   139	                    page_w_px = width_pt * dpi / 72
   140	                    page_h_px = height_pt * dpi / 72
   141	                    max_pixels = 20_000_000
   142	                    if page_w_px * page_h_px > max_pixels:
   143	                        scale = (max_pixels / (page_w_px * page_h_px)) ** 0.5
   144	                        effective_dpi = max(72, int(dpi * scale))
   145	                        logger.info("Page %d too large (%.0fx%.0f px at %d DPI), reducing to %d DPI",
   146	                                    pi, page_w_px, page_h_px, dpi, effective_dpi)
   147	                    else:
   148	                        effective_dpi = dpi
   149	
   150	                    tp = page.get_textpage_ocr(language=languages, dpi=effective_dpi, full=True)
   151	                    page_dict = page.get_text("dict", textpage=tp)
   152	                    blocks: List[BlockInfo] = []
   153	                    for blk in page_dict.get("blocks", []):
   154	                        if blk.get("type", 0) != 0:
   155	                            continue
   156	                        lines = blk.get("lines", [])
   157	                        if not lines:
   158	                            continue
   159	                        for line_info in self._extract_line_blocks(pi, lines):
   160	                            blocks.append(line_info)
   161	
   162	                    pages.append(
   163	                        PageInfo(
   164	                            page_number=pi,
   165	                            width_pt=width_pt,
   166	                            height_pt=height_pt,
   167	                            rotation=rotation,
   168	                            blocks=blocks,
   169	                        )
   170	                    )
   171	                except Exception as e:
   172	                    logger.warning("OCR failed on page %d: %s", pi, e)
   173	                    # Skip this page but continue with the rest
   174	                    continue
   175	            return pages
   176	        finally:
   177	            doc.close()
   178	
   179	    def detect_scanned(self, pages: Sequence[PageInfo]) -> bool:
   180	        """Return True if the document appears to be scanned (no text layer).
   181	
   182	        Heuristic: if >70% of pages have text length <50 chars, mark as scanned.
   183	        """
   184	        if not pages:
   185	            return True
   186	        low_text_pages = 0
   187	        for p in pages:
   188	            total_len = sum(len(b.text) for b in p.blocks)
   189	            if total_len < 50:
   190	                low_text_pages += 1
   191	        return (low_text_pages / max(1, len(pages))) > 0.70
   192	
   193	    def clean_text_blocks(
   194	        self,
   195	        blocks: Sequence[BlockInfo],
   196	        page_width: float,
   197	        page_height: float,
   198	        header_texts: Optional[set[str]] = None,
   199	        footer_texts: Optional[set[str]] = None,
   200	    ) -> List[CleanBlock]:
   201	        """Remove common header/footer texts and fix hyphenations.
   202	
   203	        - Removes any block whose text matches a commonly repeated header/footer
   204	          phrase that appears in the top/bottom 10% region across >60% pages.
   205	        - Returns cleaned blocks with basic normalization.
   206	        """
   207	        header_texts = header_texts or set()
   208	        footer_texts = footer_texts or set()
   209	        top_cutoff = page_height * self.HEADER_FOOTER_REGION_RATIO
   210	        bottom_cutoff = page_height * (1.0 - self.HEADER_FOOTER_REGION_RATIO)
   211	
   212	        cleaned: List[CleanBlock] = []
   213	        for b in blocks:
   214	            x0, y0, x1, y1 = b.bbox
   215	            is_top = y0 <= top_cutoff
   216	            is_bottom = y1 >= bottom_cutoff
   217	            text_norm = self._normalize_inline_text(b.text)
   218	
   219	            if is_top and text_norm in header_texts:
   220	                continue
   221	            if is_bottom and text_norm in footer_texts:
   222	                continue
   223	
   224	            cleaned.append(CleanBlock(page=b.page, text=text_norm, bbox=b.bbox, font_size=b.font_size))
   225	
   226	        return cleaned
   227	
   228	    def chunk_document(self, pages: Sequence[PageInfo]) -> List[ChunkInfo]:
   229	        """Produce ChunkInfo items with normalized bboxes and metadata.
   230	
   231	        Steps:
   232	        1) Identify repeated header/footer texts across pages
   233	        2) Clean blocks per page (remove header/footer, fix hyphenations)
   234	        3) Detect headings via font size threshold (size > median × 1.3)
   235	        4) Split into sentence-level units, then assemble chunks sized 300–500 tokens
   236	           with 50-token overlap
   237	        5) Normalize bbox coordinates to [0,1] using page dims (top-left origin)
   238	        """
   239	        # 1) Header/footer detection
   240	        header_texts, footer_texts = self._detect_header_footer_texts(pages)
   241	
   242	        # 2) Clean and collect blocks, also gather font sizes for median
   243	        all_clean_blocks: List[CleanBlock] = []
   244	        page_dims: dict[int, Tuple[float, float]] = {}
   245	        for p in pages:
   246	            page_dims[p.page_number] = (p.width_pt, p.height_pt)
   247	            cleaned = self.clean_text_blocks(
   248	                p.blocks, p.width_pt, p.height_pt, header_texts=header_texts, footer_texts=footer_texts
   249	            )
   250	            all_clean_blocks.extend(self._order_blocks_for_reading(cleaned, p.width_pt, p.height_pt))
   251	
   252	        if not all_clean_blocks:
   253	            return []
   254	
   255	        font_sizes = [cb.font_size for cb in all_clean_blocks if cb.font_size > 0]
   256	        median_size = median(font_sizes) if font_sizes else 12.0
   257	
   258	        # 3) Determine headings and build content sentences
   259	        current_section_title: Optional[str] = None
   260	        # (text, page, bbox, section_title)
   261	        sentences: List[tuple[str, int, Tuple[float, float, float, float], Optional[str]]] = []
   262	
   263	        for cb in all_clean_blocks:
   264	            if self._is_heading_block(cb, median_size):
   265	                # Heading: update current section title; do not include in content
   266	                title = cb.text.strip()
   267	                # Avoid overly long titles
   268	                current_section_title = title[:200] if title else None
   269	                continue
   270	
   271	            # Treat each block as a paragraph, split it into sentences
   272	            for sent in self._split_into_sentences(cb.text):
   273	                st = sent.strip()
   274	                if not st:
   275	                    continue
   276	                sentences.append((st, cb.page, cb.bbox, current_section_title))
   277	
   278	        # 4) Assemble chunks by sentences with overlap
   279	        chunks: List[ChunkInfo] = []
   280	        if not sentences:
   281	            return chunks
   282	
   283	        # Build parallel token counts for each sentence
   284	        sent_tokens = [self._estimate_tokens(t) for (t, _pg, _bb, _sec) in sentences]
   285	
   286	        start_idx = 0
   287	        chunk_index = 0
   288	
   289	        while start_idx < len(sentences):
   290	            token_sum = 0
   291	            end_idx = start_idx
   292	
   293	            # Expand until reaching the target range
   294	            while end_idx < len(sentences) and token_sum < self.TARGET_MIN_TOKENS:
   295	                token_sum += sent_tokens[end_idx]
   296	                end_idx += 1
   297	
   298	            # If we can still add more sentences without exceeding max, do so
   299	            while end_idx < len(sentences) and (token_sum + sent_tokens[end_idx]) <= self.TARGET_MAX_TOKENS:
   300	                token_sum += sent_tokens[end_idx]
   301	                end_idx += 1
   302	
   303	            # Fallback: ensure at least one sentence
   304	            if end_idx == start_idx:
   305	                end_idx = min(start_idx + 1, len(sentences))
   306	                token_sum = sum(sent_tokens[start_idx:end_idx])
   307	
   308	            # Aggregate text and bboxes
   309	            sel = sentences[start_idx:end_idx]
   310	            text = self._join_text_units([s for (s, _pg, _bb, _sec) in sel])
   311	            pages_range = [pg for (_t, pg, _bb, _sec) in sel]
   312	            page_start = min(pages_range) if pages_range else 1
   313	            page_end = max(pages_range) if pages_range else page_start
   314	
   315	            # Aggregate and normalize bboxes per sentence
   316	            bbox_dicts: List[dict] = []
   317	            for (_t, pg, bb, _sec) in sel:
   318	                w, h = page_dims.get(pg, (1.0, 1.0))
   319	                bbox_dicts.append(self._normalize_bbox(pg, bb, w, h))
   320	
   321	            # Track section title for this chunk as the last known heading before start_idx
   322	            # We approximate by scanning backwards to find the last heading block before start_idx
   323	            # Since we do not store headings separately per position, we reuse last_known_title
   324	            section_title = sel[0][3] if sel and sel[0][3] else None
   325	
   326	            chunks.append(
   327	                ChunkInfo(
   328	                    text=text,
   329	                    chunk_index=chunk_index,
   330	                    page_start=page_start,
   331	                    page_end=page_end,
   332	                    bboxes=bbox_dicts,
   333	                    section_title=section_title,
   334	                    token_count=self._estimate_tokens(text),
   335	                )
   336	            )
   337	            chunk_index += 1
   338	
   339	            # Advance start index with overlap of 50 tokens
   340	            # Compute how many sentences to step back to preserve ~50 tokens overlap
   341	            overlap = self.OVERLAP_TOKENS
   342	            # We want next window to start so that the tail has ~overlap tokens
   343	            # Find k: sum of last k sentences' tokens >= overlap
   344	            k = 0
   345	            acc = 0
   346	            j = end_idx - 1
   347	            while j >= start_idx and acc < overlap:
   348	                acc += sent_tokens[j]
   349	                k += 1
   350	                j -= 1
   351	            next_start = max(end_idx - k, start_idx + 1)  # ensure progress
   352	
   353	            start_idx = next_start
   354	
   355	        # Post-process: remove micro-chunks that provide no retrieval value.
   356	        # These arise from form fields, metadata footers, or single short lines.
   357	        MIN_CHUNK_CHARS = 50
   358	        filtered: List[ChunkInfo] = []
   359	        pending_micro: List[ChunkInfo] = []
   360	        for c in chunks:
   361	            if len(c.text.strip()) >= MIN_CHUNK_CHARS:
   362	                if pending_micro:
   363	                    prefix = pending_micro[0]
   364	                    for micro in pending_micro[1:]:
   365	                        prefix = self._merge_adjacent_chunks(prefix, micro)
   366	                    c = self._merge_adjacent_chunks(prefix, c)
   367	                    pending_micro = []
   368	                filtered.append(c)
   369	            elif filtered:
   370	                # Merge micro-chunk text into previous chunk
   371	                prev = filtered[-1]
   372	                filtered[-1] = self._merge_adjacent_chunks(prev, c)
   373	            else:
   374	                pending_micro.append(c)
   375	
   376	        if pending_micro and not filtered:
   377	            # Short documents still need a searchable chunk; do not filter the
   378	            # whole document down to nothing.
   379	            combined = pending_micro[0]
   380	            for micro in pending_micro[1:]:
   381	                combined = self._merge_adjacent_chunks(combined, micro)
   382	            filtered.append(combined)
   383	
   384	        # Re-index chunk_index after filtering
   385	        for i, c in enumerate(filtered):
   386	            if c.chunk_index != i:
   387	                filtered[i] = ChunkInfo(
   388	                    text=c.text,
   389	                    chunk_index=i,
   390	                    page_start=c.page_start,
   391	                    page_end=c.page_end,
   392	                    bboxes=c.bboxes,
   393	                    section_title=c.section_title,
   394	                    token_count=c.token_count,
   395	                )
   396	
   397	        return filtered
   398	
   399	    def extract_elements(self, pages: Sequence[PageInfo]) -> List[ElementInfo]:
   400	        """Build a canonical reading-order element stream from parsed pages.
   401	
   402	        Chunks remain the citation anchor for existing RAG. Elements are a
   403	        higher-level document model used by summary, extraction, diff, and
   404	        table-aware retrieval so those workflows do not have to infer document
   405	        structure from arbitrary chunk windows.
   406	        """
   407	        header_texts, footer_texts = self._detect_header_footer_texts(pages)
   408	
   409	        all_clean_blocks: List[CleanBlock] = []
   410	        page_dims: dict[int, Tuple[float, float]] = {}
   411	        for p in pages:
   412	            page_dims[p.page_number] = (p.width_pt, p.height_pt)
   413	            cleaned = self.clean_text_blocks(
   414	                p.blocks,
   415	                p.width_pt,
   416	                p.height_pt,
   417	                header_texts=header_texts,
   418	                footer_texts=footer_texts,
   419	            )
   420	            all_clean_blocks.extend(self._order_blocks_for_reading(cleaned, p.width_pt, p.height_pt))

    10	from app.models.tables import Chunk, Document, DocumentBrief
    11	from app.services.document_element_service import (
    12	    chunk_to_retrieval_item,
    13	    get_element_aware_chunks_async,
    14	)
    15	
    16	MIN_SUMMARY_CHUNK_CHARS = 80
    17	DEFAULT_MAX_SUMMARY_CHUNKS = 18
    18	DEFAULT_MAX_COLLECTION_SUMMARY_CHUNKS = 24
    19	DEFAULT_MAX_COLLECTION_SUMMARY_DOCS = 8
    20	
    21	
    22	def _chunk_text_length(chunk: Any) -> int:
    23	    return len((getattr(chunk, "text", "") or "").strip())
    24	
    25	
    26	def _select_representative_chunks(
    27	    chunks: Sequence[Any],
    28	    *,
    29	    max_chunks: int = DEFAULT_MAX_SUMMARY_CHUNKS,
    30	) -> list[Any]:
    31	    """Select ordered chunks for broad document coverage.
    32	
    33	    This deliberately does not use query similarity. Whole-document summaries
    34	    need beginning/middle/end coverage and section diversity, while semantic
    35	    top-k often over-selects tables, appendices, or repeated sidebars for vague
    36	    prompts like "summarize this document".
    37	    """
    38	    usable = [
    39	        ch for ch in chunks
    40	        if _chunk_text_length(ch) >= MIN_SUMMARY_CHUNK_CHARS
    41	    ]
    42	    if not usable:
    43	        return list(chunks[:max_chunks])
    44	    if len(usable) <= max_chunks:
    45	        return list(usable)
    46	
    47	    selected_indices: set[int] = set()
    48	
    49	    # Preserve early orientation: title, thesis, intro, abstract often appear
    50	    # near the front, but cap it to avoid front-loaded summaries.
    51	    front_count = min(4, max_chunks // 3)
    52	    selected_indices.update(range(front_count))
    53	
    54	    # Keep likely conclusion / appendix tail coverage without over-weighting it.
    55	    selected_indices.update(range(max(0, len(usable) - 2), len(usable)))
    56	
    57	    # Add chunks when a new section title appears. This recovers broad structure
    58	    # cheaply until a durable section index exists.
    59	    seen_sections: set[str] = set()
    60	    for idx, chunk in enumerate(usable):
    61	        section = (getattr(chunk, "section_title", None) or "").strip().lower()
    62	        if not section or section in seen_sections:
    63	            continue
    64	        seen_sections.add(section)
    65	        selected_indices.add(idx)
    66	        if len(selected_indices) >= max_chunks:
    67	            break
    68	
    69	    # Fill remaining budget with evenly spaced coverage.
    70	    remaining = max_chunks - len(selected_indices)
    71	    if remaining > 0:
    72	        if remaining == 1:
    73	            selected_indices.add(len(usable) // 2)
    74	        else:
    75	            for slot in range(remaining):
    76	                idx = round(slot * (len(usable) - 1) / (remaining - 1))
    77	                selected_indices.add(idx)
    78	
    79	    # If section titles consumed too much budget, keep deterministic coverage:
    80	    # earliest orientation, evenly-spaced body, tail.
    81	    if len(selected_indices) > max_chunks:
    82	        fixed = set(range(front_count))
    83	        fixed.update(range(max(0, len(usable) - 2), len(usable)))
    84	        middle_budget = max_chunks - len(fixed)
    85	        middle = [
    86	            idx for idx in selected_indices
    87	            if idx not in fixed
    88	        ]
    89	        if middle_budget > 0 and middle:
    90	            step = max(1, math.ceil(len(middle) / middle_budget))
    91	            fixed.update(middle[::step][:middle_budget])
    92	        selected_indices = set(sorted(fixed)[:max_chunks])
    93	
    94	    return [usable[idx] for idx in sorted(selected_indices)[:max_chunks]]
    95	
    96	
    97	def _chunk_to_retrieval_item(chunk: Chunk, score: float) -> dict[str, Any]:
    98	    return {
    99	        "chunk_id": chunk.id,
   100	        "text": chunk.text,
   101	        "page": chunk.page_start,
   102	        "page_end": chunk.page_end,
   103	        "bboxes": chunk.bboxes,
   104	        "score": score,
   105	        "section_title": chunk.section_title,
   106	        "document_id": chunk.document_id,
   107	    }
   108	
   109	
   110	class DocumentBriefService:
   111	    async def get_summary_context(
   112	        self,
   113	        db: AsyncSession,
   114	        document_id: uuid.UUID,
   115	        *,
   116	        max_chunks: int = DEFAULT_MAX_SUMMARY_CHUNKS,
   117	    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   118	        persisted = await self._get_persisted_summary_context(
   119	            db,
   120	            document_id,
   121	            max_chunks=max_chunks,
   122	        )
   123	        if persisted:
   124	            return persisted
   125	
   126	        element_chunks = await get_element_aware_chunks_async(
   127	            db,
   128	            document_id,
   129	            max_chunks=max_chunks,
   130	        )
   131	        if element_chunks:
   132	            return [
   133	                chunk_to_retrieval_item(chunk, score, include_document_id=True)
   134	                for chunk, score in element_chunks
   135	            ]
   136	
   137	        rows = await db.execute(
   138	            select(Chunk)
   139	            .where(Chunk.document_id == document_id)
   140	            .order_by(Chunk.chunk_index)
   141	        )
   142	        chunks = list(rows.scalars())
   143	        selected = _select_representative_chunks(chunks, max_chunks=max_chunks)
   144	        total = max(1, len(selected))
   145	        return [
   146	            _chunk_to_retrieval_item(chunk, 1.0 - (idx / (total + 1)) * 0.2)
   147	            for idx, chunk in enumerate(selected)
   148	        ]
   149	
   150	    async def _get_persisted_summary_context(
   151	        self,
   152	        db: AsyncSession,
   153	        document_id: uuid.UUID,
   154	        *,
   155	        max_chunks: int,
   156	    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   157	        brief_row = await db.execute(
   158	            select(DocumentBrief.coverage).where(DocumentBrief.document_id == document_id)
   159	        )
   160	        coverage = brief_row.scalar_one_or_none()
   161	        if not isinstance(coverage, dict):
   162	            return []
   163	        raw_ids = coverage.get("selected_chunk_ids")
   164	        if not isinstance(raw_ids, list) or not raw_ids:
   165	            return []
   166	
   167	        chunk_ids: list[uuid.UUID] = []
   168	        for raw_id in raw_ids[:max_chunks]:
   169	            try:
   170	                chunk_ids.append(uuid.UUID(str(raw_id)))
   171	            except (TypeError, ValueError):
   172	                continue
   173	        if not chunk_ids:
   174	            return []
   175	
   176	        rows = await db.execute(
   177	            select(Chunk)
   178	            .where(Chunk.document_id == document_id)
   179	            .where(Chunk.id.in_(chunk_ids))
   180	        )
   181	        chunks_by_id = {chunk.id: chunk for chunk in rows.scalars()}
   182	        ordered = [
   183	            chunks_by_id[chunk_id]
   184	            for chunk_id in chunk_ids
   185	            if chunk_id in chunks_by_id
   186	        ]
   187	        total = max(1, len(ordered))
   188	        return [
   189	            _chunk_to_retrieval_item(chunk, 1.0 - (idx / (total + 1)) * 0.2)
   190	            for idx, chunk in enumerate(ordered)
   191	        ]
   192	
   193	    async def get_collection_summary_context(
   194	        self,
   195	        db: AsyncSession,
   196	        document_ids: Sequence[uuid.UUID],
   197	        *,
   198	        max_chunks: int = DEFAULT_MAX_COLLECTION_SUMMARY_CHUNKS,
   199	        max_docs: int = DEFAULT_MAX_COLLECTION_SUMMARY_DOCS,
   200	    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   201	        selected_doc_ids = list(document_ids[:max_docs])
   202	        if not selected_doc_ids:
   203	            return []
   204	
   205	        per_doc_budget = max(1, max_chunks // len(selected_doc_ids))
   206	        contexts: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   207	        for doc_id in selected_doc_ids:
   208	            contexts.extend(
   209	                await self.get_summary_context(
   210	                    db,
   211	                    doc_id,
   212	                    max_chunks=per_doc_budget,
   213	                )
   214	            )
   215	            if len(contexts) >= max_chunks:
   216	                break
   217	        return contexts[:max_chunks]
   218	
   219	    async def get_document_label(self, db: AsyncSession, document_id: uuid.UUID) -> str:
   220	        doc = await db.get(Document, document_id)
   221	        return doc.filename if doc else "document"
   222	
   223	
   224	document_brief_service = DocumentBriefService()

   114	def _is_deepseek_official_model(model: str) -> bool:
   115	    return model in settings.DEEPSEEK_OFFICIAL_MODELS

   117	
   118	def _get_llm_client(model: str) -> OpenAI:
   119	    if _is_deepseek_official_model(model):
   120	        if not settings.DEEPSEEK_API_KEY:
   121	            raise RuntimeError("DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is not configured")
   122	        return OpenAI(api_key=settings.DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, base_url=settings.DEEPSEEK_BASE_URL)
   123	    if not settings.OPENROUTER_API_KEY:

   128	def _apply_provider_options(kwargs: dict[str, Any], model: str) -> None:

   130	        kwargs["extra_body"] = {"thinking": {"type": "disabled"}}
   131	
   132	
   133	def _json_from_text(text: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
   134	    content = (text or "").strip()
   135	    if content.startswith("```"):
   136	        content = re.sub(r"^```(?:json)?\s*", "", content)
   137	        content = re.sub(r"\s*```$", "", content)
   138	    try:
   139	        data = json.loads(content)
   140	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
   141	        match = re.search(r"\{.*\}", content, flags=re.DOTALL)
   142	        if not match:
   143	            raise
   144	        data = json.loads(match.group(0))
   145	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
   146	        raise ValueError("Extraction response must be a JSON object")
   147	    return data
   148	
   149	
   150	def _valid_bbox(bb: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   151	    return all(isinstance(bb.get(k), (int, float)) for k in ("x", "y", "w", "h"))
   152	
   153	
   154	def _citation_from_chunk(ref_num: int, chunk: Chunk, score: float = 0.0) -> dict[str, Any]:
   155	    bboxes = [
   156	        bb for bb in (chunk.bboxes or [])
   157	        if isinstance(bb, dict) and _valid_bbox(bb)
   158	    ]
   159	    bboxes.sort(
   160	        key=lambda bb: (
   161	            int(bb.get("page", chunk.page_start)) if isinstance(bb.get("page", chunk.page_start), (int, float)) else chunk.page_start,
   162	            bb.get("y", 0),
   163	            bb.get("x", 0),
   164	        )
   165	    )
   166	    page_counts: dict[int, int] = {}
   167	    for bb in bboxes:
   168	        raw_page = bb.get("page", chunk.page_start)
   169	        page = int(raw_page) if isinstance(raw_page, (int, float)) else chunk.page_start
   170	        page_counts[page] = page_counts.get(page, 0) + 1
   171	    best_page = min(page_counts, key=lambda p: (-page_counts[p], p)) if page_counts else chunk.page_start
   172	    snippet = ((f"{chunk.section_title}: " if chunk.section_title else "") + (chunk.text or ""))[:140]
   173	    return {
   174	        "ref_index": ref_num,
   175	        "chunk_id": str(chunk.id),
   176	        "page": best_page,
   177	        "page_end": chunk.page_end,
   178	        "bboxes": bboxes,
   179	        "text_snippet": snippet,
   180	        "offset": 0,
   181	        "confidence_score": round(float(score or 0.0), 3),
   182	        "context_text": (chunk.text or "")[:300],
   183	        "document_id": str(chunk.document_id),
   184	    }
   185	
   186	
   187	def _retrieve_by_query(db: Session, document_id: uuid.UUID, query: str, top_k: int) -> list[tuple[Chunk, float]]:
   188	    try:
   189	        qvec = embedding_service.embed_texts([query])[0]
   190	        client = embedding_service.get_qdrant_client()
   191	        response = client.query_points(
   192	            collection_name=settings.QDRANT_COLLECTION,
   193	            query=qvec,
   194	            limit=max(top_k * 3, top_k),
   195	            query_filter=Filter(must=[FieldCondition(key="document_id", match=MatchValue(value=str(document_id)))]),
   196	        )
   197	        scores: dict[uuid.UUID, float] = {}
   198	        ids: list[uuid.UUID] = []
   199	        for point in response.points:
   200	            try:
   201	                cid = uuid.UUID(str(point.id))
   202	            except Exception:
   203	                continue
   204	            ids.append(cid)
   205	            scores[cid] = float(point.score or 0.0)
   206	        if not ids:
   207	            return []
   208	        rows = db.execute(select(Chunk).where(Chunk.id.in_(ids)))
   209	        chunks = list(rows.scalars())
   210	        chunks.sort(key=lambda ch: scores.get(ch.id, 0.0), reverse=True)
   211	        return [(ch, scores.get(ch.id, 0.0)) for ch in chunks if len((ch.text or "").strip()) >= 80][:top_k]
   212	    except Exception as exc:
   213	        logger.warning("Extraction vector retrieval failed, falling back to first chunks: %s", exc)
   214	        return []
   215	
   216	
   217	def retrieve_extraction_chunks(
   218	    db: Session,
   219	    document_id: uuid.UUID,
   220	    template: ExtractionTemplate,
   221	    *,
   222	    max_chunks: int = MAX_CONTEXT_CHUNKS,
   223	) -> list[tuple[Chunk, float]]:
   224	    seen: set[uuid.UUID] = set()
   225	    selected: list[tuple[Chunk, float]] = []
   226	    element_budget = max(2, max_chunks // 2)
   227	    for chunk, score in get_element_aware_chunks(db, document_id, max_chunks=element_budget):
   228	        if chunk.id in seen:
   229	            continue
   230	        seen.add(chunk.id)
   231	        selected.append((chunk, score))
   232	        if len(selected) >= max_chunks:
   233	            return selected
   234	
   235	    per_query = max(3, max_chunks // max(1, len(template.query_prompts)) + 1)
   236	    for query in template.query_prompts:
   237	        for chunk, score in _retrieve_by_query(db, document_id, query, per_query):
   238	            if chunk.id in seen:
   239	                continue
   240	            seen.add(chunk.id)
   241	            selected.append((chunk, score))
   242	            if len(selected) >= max_chunks:
   243	                return selected
   244	
   245	    if selected:
   246	        return selected[:max_chunks]
   247	
   248	    rows = db.execute(
   249	        select(Chunk)
   250	        .where(Chunk.document_id == document_id)
   251	        .order_by(Chunk.chunk_index)
   252	        .limit(max_chunks)
   253	    )
   254	    return [(chunk, 0.0) for chunk in rows.scalars()]
   255	
   256	
   257	def _context_text(chunks: Sequence[tuple[Chunk, float]]) -> str:
   258	    parts: list[str] = []
   259	    for idx, (chunk, _score) in enumerate(chunks, start=1):
   260	        text = (chunk.text or "").strip().replace("\x00", "")
   261	        if len(text) > MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS_PER_CHUNK:
   262	            text = text[:MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS_PER_CHUNK] + "..."
   263	        section = f" | section: {chunk.section_title}" if chunk.section_title else ""
   264	        parts.append(f"[{idx}] page {chunk.page_start}{section}\n{text}")
   265	    return "\n\n".join(parts)
   266	
   267	
   268	def _system_prompt(template: ExtractionTemplate, domain_mode: str | None) -> str:
   269	    domain = f"\nDomain mode: {domain_mode}." if domain_mode else ""
   270	    return (
   271	        "You are DocTalk's structured extraction engine. Extract only facts supported by the provided document fragments. "
   272	        "Every extracted item must include source_refs using the bracket numbers of the fragments that support it. "
   273	        "Do not invent facts. Respond only with valid JSON matching this contract:\n"
   274	        f"{template.json_contract}\n"
   275	        f"{domain}"
   276	    )
   277	
   278	
   279	def _user_prompt(template: ExtractionTemplate, chunks: Sequence[tuple[Chunk, float]], locale: str | None) -> str:
   280	    language_rule = f"Use the user's interface language if clear from this locale: {locale}." if locale else "Use the document language."
   281	    return (
   282	        f"Template: {template.title}\n"
   283	        f"Goal: {template.description}\n"
   284	        f"{language_rule}\n\n"
   285	        "Document fragments:\n"
   286	        f"{_context_text(chunks)}"
   287	    )
   288	
   289	
   290	def _call_llm(template: ExtractionTemplate, chunks: Sequence[tuple[Chunk, float]], locale: str | None, domain_mode: str | None) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], int, int]:
   291	    client = _get_llm_client(EXTRACTION_MODEL)
   292	    messages = [
   293	        {"role": "system", "content": _system_prompt(template, domain_mode)},
   294	        {"role": "user", "content": _user_prompt(template, chunks, locale)},
   295	    ]
   296	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {
   297	        "model": EXTRACTION_MODEL,
   298	        "messages": messages,
   299	        "temperature": 0.1,
   300	        "max_tokens": 1800,
   301	    }
   302	    _apply_provider_options(kwargs, EXTRACTION_MODEL)
   303	    response = client.chat.completions.create(**kwargs)
   304	    content = response.choices[0].message.content or ""
   305	    usage = getattr(response, "usage", None)
   306	    prompt_tokens = int(getattr(usage, "prompt_tokens", 0) or 0)
   307	    completion_tokens = int(getattr(usage, "completion_tokens", 0) or 0)
   308	    try:
   309	        return _json_from_text(content), prompt_tokens, completion_tokens
   310	    except Exception:
   311	        repair_messages = [
   312	            {
   313	                "role": "system",
   314	                "content": "Repair the following model output into valid JSON only. Do not add commentary.",
   315	            },
   316	            {
   317	                "role": "user",
   318	                "content": f"Required contract:\n{template.json_contract}\n\nOutput:\n{content}",
   319	            },
   320	        ]
   321	        repair_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {
   322	            "model": EXTRACTION_MODEL,
   323	            "messages": repair_messages,
   324	            "temperature": 0,
   325	            "max_tokens": 1800,
   326	        }
   327	        _apply_provider_options(repair_kwargs, EXTRACTION_MODEL)
   328	        repaired = client.chat.completions.create(**repair_kwargs)
   329	        repaired_content = repaired.choices[0].message.content or ""
   330	        repair_usage = getattr(repaired, "usage", None)
   331	        prompt_tokens += int(getattr(repair_usage, "prompt_tokens", 0) or 0)
   332	        completion_tokens += int(getattr(repair_usage, "completion_tokens", 0) or 0)
   333	        return _json_from_text(repaired_content), prompt_tokens, completion_tokens
   334	
   335	
   336	def _refs(value: Any, max_ref: int) -> list[int]:
   337	    if not isinstance(value, list):
   338	        return [1] if max_ref >= 1 else []
   339	    refs: list[int] = []
   340	    for item in value:
   341	        try:
   342	            ref = int(item)
   343	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
   344	            continue
   345	        if 1 <= ref <= max_ref and ref not in refs:
   346	            refs.append(ref)
   347	    return refs or ([1] if max_ref >= 1 else [])
   348	
   349	
   350	def _str(value: Any, fallback: str = "") -> str:
   351	    text = str(value).strip() if value is not None else fallback
   352	    return text[:4000]
   353	
   354	
   355	def normalize_result(template_key: str, raw: dict[str, Any], max_ref: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
   356	    if template_key == "executive_summary":
   357	        key_points = raw.get("key_points") if isinstance(raw.get("key_points"), list) else []
   358	        risks = raw.get("risks_or_open_questions") if isinstance(raw.get("risks_or_open_questions"), list) else []
   359	        return {
   360	            "title": _str(raw.get("title"), "Executive Summary")[:200],

   446	                    batch = chunks[i : i + batch_size]
   447	                    texts = [c.text for c in batch]

我补了一次运行时确认：当前解释器里 `asyncio.CancelledError` 的 MRO 确实是 `BaseException` 分支，不会被 `except Exception` 捕获。接下来我会查项目里是否有额外的全局取消兜底。

    51	@asynccontextmanager
    52	async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    53	    import logging
    54	    import threading
    55	
    56	    # Configure startup logger with a handler so messages actually appear.
    57	    # Python's lastResort handler only outputs WARNING+; we need INFO for
    58	    # "Found N stuck documents" and other startup diagnostics.
    59	    logger = logging.getLogger("doctalk.startup")
    60	    if not logger.handlers:
    61	        handler = logging.StreamHandler()
    62	        handler.setFormatter(logging.Formatter("[%(name)s] %(message)s"))
    63	        logger.addHandler(handler)
    64	        logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
    65	
    66	    def _alert(exc: Exception, context: str) -> None:
    67	        """Log an error with traceback and send to Sentry if configured."""
    68	        logger.error("%s: %s", context, exc, exc_info=True)
    69	        if settings.SENTRY_DSN:
    70	            try:
    71	                import sentry_sdk
    72	                sentry_sdk.capture_exception(exc)
    73	            except Exception:
    74	                pass
    75	
    76	    def _is_already_exists(exc: Exception) -> bool:
    77	        """Detect Qdrant 409 Conflict (index/collection already exists).
    78	
    79	        qdrant-client raises UnexpectedResponse with .status_code for REST
    80	        non-2xx; fall back to 409 substring only if the attribute is absent.
    81	        """
    82	        status = getattr(exc, "status_code", None)
    83	        if isinstance(status, int):
    84	            return status == 409
    85	        # Fallback for wrappers that don't expose status_code
    86	        msg = str(exc)
    87	        return "409" in msg
    88	
    89	    def _init_services() -> None:
    90	        try:
    91	            storage_service.ensure_bucket()
    92	            logger.info("MinIO bucket ready")
    93	        except Exception as e:
    94	            _alert(e, "MinIO bucket ensure failed at startup")
    95	        try:
    96	            embedding_service.ensure_collection()
    97	            logger.info("Qdrant collection ready")
    98	            try:
    99	                qdrant = embedding_service.get_qdrant_client()
   100	                qdrant.create_payload_index(
   101	                    collection_name=settings.QDRANT_COLLECTION,
   102	                    field_name="document_id",
   103	                    field_schema=PayloadSchemaType.KEYWORD,
   104	                )
   105	                logger.info("Qdrant payload index ready for field=document_id")
   106	            except Exception as e:
   107	                # 409 = already exists (expected on restart, not actionable).
   108	                # Any other failure degrades retrievals — alert.
   109	                if _is_already_exists(e):
   110	                    logger.info("Qdrant payload index already exists (skipping)")
   111	                else:
   112	                    _alert(e, "Qdrant payload index create failed")
   113	        except Exception as e:
   114	            # Missing collection = all retrievals fail. Must alert.
   115	            _alert(e, "Qdrant collection ensure failed")
   116	
   117	    def _retry_stuck_documents() -> None:
   118	        """Re-dispatch parse tasks for documents stuck in 'parsing' status."""
   119	        try:
   120	            from sqlalchemy import select
   121	
   122	            from app.models.sync_database import SyncSessionLocal
   123	            from app.models.tables import Document
   124	
   125	            with SyncSessionLocal() as db:
   126	                rows = db.execute(
   127	                    select(Document).where(Document.status.in_(["parsing", "ocr", "embedding"]))
   128	                )
   129	                stuck = list(rows.scalars())
   130	                if not stuck:
   131	                    return
   132	                logger.info("Found %d stuck documents, re-dispatching parse tasks", len(stuck))
   133	                from app.workers.parse_worker import parse_document
   134	                for doc in stuck:
   135	                    try:
   136	                        parse_document.delay(str(doc.id))
   137	                        logger.info("Re-dispatched parse for %s (%s)", doc.id, doc.filename)
   138	                    except Exception as e:
   139	                        logger.warning("Failed to re-dispatch parse for %s: %s", doc.id, e)
   140	        except Exception as e:
   141	            logger.warning("Stuck document retry failed: %s", e)
   142	
   143	    def _seed_demo_documents() -> None:
   144	        try:
   145	            from app.services.demo_seed import seed_demo_documents
   146	            seed_demo_documents()
   147	        except Exception as e:
   148	            logger.warning("Demo document seeding failed: %s", e)
   149	
   150	    # Run service initialization in background so app starts immediately
   151	    def _startup_tasks() -> None:
   152	        _init_services()
   153	        _retry_stuck_documents()
   154	        _seed_demo_documents()
   155	
   156	    t = threading.Thread(target=_startup_tasks, daemon=True)
   157	    t.start()
   158	    yield

   165	sentry_environment = (settings.SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT or "").lower()

   167	cors_origins = [settings.FRONTEND_URL] if is_production else [
   168	    "http://localhost:3000",

   204	@app.get("/version", response_model=ReleaseInfo)
   205	async def version() -> dict:
   206	    return get_release_payload()
   207	
   208	
   209	@app.get("/health", response_model=Union[HealthDeepResponse, HealthResponse])
   210	async def health(request: Request, deep: bool = Query(False)) -> dict:
   211	    release = get_release_payload()
   212	    if not deep:
   213	        return {"status": "ok", "release": release}
   214	
   215	    # Require shared secret for deep health checks to prevent info leakage
   216	    expected = settings.ADAPTER_SECRET
   217	    provided = request.headers.get("x-health-secret")
   218	    if not expected or not provided or not hmac.compare_digest(provided, expected):
   219	        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Unauthorized")
   220	
   221	    components: dict[str, dict[str, str]] = {
   222	        "database": {"status": "ok"},
   223	        "redis": {"status": "ok"},
   224	        "qdrant": {"status": "ok"},
   225	        "minio": {"status": "ok"},
   226	    }
   227	
   228	    async def _check_db() -> None:
   229	        async with AsyncSessionLocal() as db:
   230	            await db.execute(text("SELECT 1"))
   231	
   232	    async def _check_redis() -> None:
   233	        import redis.asyncio as redis
   234	
   235	        client = redis.from_url(settings.CELERY_BROKER_URL)
   236	        try:
   237	            await client.ping()
   238	        finally:

     1	from __future__ import annotations
     2	
     3	import asyncio
     4	import re

    13	from app.core.config import settings
    14	from app.models.tables import Chunk, DocumentTable
    15	from app.services.embedding_service import embedding_service
    16	from app.services.rag_evaluator_service import extract_query_terms
    17	
    18	# Minimum text length for a chunk to be useful in retrieval.
    19	# Shorter chunks are typically form fields, metadata footers, or page numbers
    20	# that pollute search results for vague queries.
    21	_MIN_CHUNK_TEXT_LEN = 200
    22	_MIN_SHORT_CHUNK_TEXT_LEN = 20
    23	_MIN_TABLE_CHUNK_TEXT_LEN = 20
    24	_TABLE_ROW_LIMIT = 12
    25	_GENERIC_TABLE_QUERY_RE = re.compile(
    26	    r"\b(table|tables|spreadsheet|csv|excel|row|rows|column|columns|cell|cells)\b"
    27	    r"|(表格|数据表|电子表格|行|列|单元格)",
    28	    flags=re.IGNORECASE,
    29	)
    30	_GENERIC_TABLE_TERMS = {
    31	    "table",
    32	    "tables",
    33	    "spreadsheet",
    34	    "csv",
    35	    "excel",
    36	    "row",
    37	    "rows",
    38	    "column",
    39	    "columns",
    40	    "cell",
    41	    "cells",
    42	}
    43	
    44	
    45	def _escape_like(term: str) -> str:
    46	    return (
    47	        term.replace("\\", "\\\\")
    48	        .replace("%", "\\%")
    49	        .replace("_", "\\_")
    50	    )
    51	
    52	
    53	def _lexical_score(text: str, terms: tuple[str, ...]) -> float:
    54	    if not terms:
    55	        return 0.0
    56	    lowered = text.lower()
    57	    matches = sum(1 for term in terms if term.lower() in lowered)
    58	    return min(0.99, 0.48 + (matches / max(len(terms), 1)) * 0.45)
    59	
    60	
    61	def _chunk_payload(ch: Chunk, *, score: float, include_document_id: bool = False) -> dict:
    62	    payload = {
    63	        "chunk_id": ch.id,
    64	        "text": ch.text,
    65	        "page": ch.page_start,
    66	        "page_end": ch.page_end,
    67	        "bboxes": ch.bboxes,
    68	        "score": score,
    69	        "section_title": ch.section_title,
    70	    }
    71	    if include_document_id:
    72	        payload["document_id"] = ch.document_id
    73	    return payload
    74	
    75	
    76	def _is_usable_chunk_text(text: str | None, *, min_text_len: int) -> bool:
    77	    return len((text or "").strip()) >= int(min_text_len)
    78	
    79	
    80	def _coerce_table_rows(table: DocumentTable) -> list[list[str]]:
    81	    rows = (table.cells or {}).get("rows")
    82	    if not isinstance(rows, list):
    83	        return []
    84	    normalized: list[list[str]] = []
    85	    for row in rows:
    86	        if not isinstance(row, list):
    87	            continue
    88	        cells = [str(cell or "").strip() for cell in row]
    89	        if any(cells):
    90	            normalized.append(cells)
    91	    return normalized
    92	
    93	
    94	def _format_table_rows(rows: list[list[str]]) -> str:
    95	    if not rows:
    96	        return ""
    97	    width = max(len(row) for row in rows)
    98	    padded = [row + [""] * (width - len(row)) for row in rows]
    99	    lines = ["| " + " | ".join(_sanitize_table_cell(cell) for cell in row) + " |" for row in padded]
   100	    if len(lines) >= 2:
   101	        lines.insert(1, "| " + " | ".join(["---"] * width) + " |")
   102	    return "\n".join(lines)
   103	
   104	
   105	def _sanitize_table_cell(value: str) -> str:
   106	    text = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", str(value or "").replace("\x00", " ")).strip()
   107	    return (
   108	        text.replace("|", "\\|")
   109	        .replace("[", "［")
   110	        .replace("]", "］")
   111	    )
   112	
   113	
   114	def _select_relevant_table_rows(rows: list[list[str]], terms: tuple[str, ...]) -> list[list[str]]:
   115	    if len(rows) <= _TABLE_ROW_LIMIT:
   116	        return rows
   117	    header = rows[0]
   118	    lowered_terms = tuple(term.lower() for term in terms if term)
   119	    if not lowered_terms:
   120	        return rows[:_TABLE_ROW_LIMIT]
   121	    matches = [
   122	        row
   123	        for row in rows[1:]
   124	        if any(term in " ".join(row).lower() for term in lowered_terms)
   125	    ]
   126	    if not matches:
   127	        return rows[:_TABLE_ROW_LIMIT]
   128	    return [header, *matches[: _TABLE_ROW_LIMIT - 1]]
   129	
   130	
   131	def _representative_chunk_for_table(table: DocumentTable, chunks: Iterable[Chunk]) -> Chunk | None:
   132	    same_document_chunks: list[Chunk] = []
   133	    for chunk in chunks:
   134	        if chunk.document_id != table.document_id:
   135	            continue
   136	        same_document_chunks.append(chunk)
   137	        if int(chunk.page_start or 0) <= table.page <= int(chunk.page_end or 0):
   138	            return chunk
   139	    return same_document_chunks[0] if same_document_chunks else None
   140	
   141	
   142	def _table_score(rows: list[list[str]], terms: tuple[str, ...], *, generic_table_query: bool) -> float:
   143	    if not rows:
   144	        return 0.0
   145	    text = " ".join(" ".join(row) for row in rows).lower()
   146	    if not terms:
   147	        return 0.64 if generic_table_query else 0.0
   148	    matched = sum(1 for term in terms if term.lower() in text)
   149	    if matched == 0:
   150	        only_generic_terms = all(term.lower() in _GENERIC_TABLE_TERMS for term in terms)
   151	        return 0.64 if generic_table_query and only_generic_terms else 0.0
   152	    return min(0.98, 0.58 + (matched / max(len(terms), 1)) * 0.38)
   153	
   154	
   155	def _table_payloads_from_tables(
   156	    query: str,
   157	    tables: Iterable[DocumentTable],
   158	    chunks: Iterable[Chunk],
   159	    *,
   160	    top_k: int,
   161	    include_document_id: bool = False,
   162	) -> list[dict]:
   163	    terms = extract_query_terms(query).lexical_terms
   164	    generic_table_query = bool(_GENERIC_TABLE_QUERY_RE.search(query or ""))
   165	    chunk_list = list(chunks)
   166	    ranked: list[tuple[float, DocumentTable, list[list[str]], Chunk]] = []
   167	    for table in tables:
   168	        rows = _coerce_table_rows(table)
   169	        score = _table_score(rows, terms, generic_table_query=generic_table_query)
   170	        if score <= 0:
   171	            continue
   172	        representative = _representative_chunk_for_table(table, chunk_list)
   173	        if representative is None:
   174	            continue
   175	        ranked.append((score, table, _select_relevant_table_rows(rows, terms), representative))
   176	
   177	    ranked.sort(key=lambda item: (item[0], -int(item[1].page or 0), -int(item[1].table_index or 0)), reverse=True)
   178	    payloads: list[dict] = []
   179	    for score, table, rows, chunk in ranked[: int(top_k or 4)]:
   180	        table_label = f"Table p.{table.page} #{int(table.table_index or 0) + 1}"
   181	        payload = {
   182	            "chunk_id": chunk.id,
   183	            "table_id": str(table.id),
   184	            "text": (
   185	                f"{table_label} ({table.method}, confidence {float(table.confidence or 0):.2f})\n"
   186	                + _format_table_rows(rows)
   187	            ),
   188	            "page": int(table.page or chunk.page_start or 1),
   189	            "page_end": int(table.page or chunk.page_end or chunk.page_start or 1),
   190	            "bboxes": [],
   191	            "score": score,
   192	            "section_title": table_label,
   193	            "retrieval_modality": "table",
   194	        }
   195	        if include_document_id:
   196	            payload["document_id"] = table.document_id
   197	        payloads.append(payload)
   198	    return payloads
   199	
   200	
   201	def table_evidence_text(table: DocumentTable) -> str:
   202	    rows = _coerce_table_rows(table)
   203	    table_label = f"Table p.{table.page} #{int(table.table_index or 0) + 1}"
   204	    return (
   205	        f"{table_label} ({table.method}, confidence {float(table.confidence or 0):.2f})\n"
   206	        + _format_table_rows(rows[:_TABLE_ROW_LIMIT])
   207	    )
   208	
   209	
   210	def _term_match_condition(term: str):
   211	    escaped = _escape_like(term)
   212	    pattern = f"%{escaped}%"
   213	    return sa.or_(
   214	        Chunk.text.ilike(pattern, escape="\\"),
   215	        Chunk.section_title.ilike(pattern, escape="\\"),
   216	    )
   217	
   218	
   219	def _lexical_query_parts(query: str):
   220	    evidence_terms = extract_query_terms(query)

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba backend/app/services/credit_service.py | sed -n '1,280p'" in /Users/mayijie/Projects/Code/010_DocTalk
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	from __future__ import annotations
     2	
     3	from typing import Optional
     4	from uuid import UUID
     5	
     6	import sqlalchemy as sa
     7	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
     8	
     9	from app.core.config import settings
    10	from app.models.tables import CreditLedger, UsageRecord, User
    11	
    12	# Token-to-credit rates by model tier
    13	CREDIT_RATES = {
    14	    # Active models (reachable via mode system)
    15	    "deepseek-v4-flash": (1, 3),
    16	    "deepseek-v4-pro": (2, 6),
    17	    "deepseek/deepseek-v3.2": (1, 5),
    18	    "mistralai/mistral-medium-3.1": (2, 10),
    19	    "mistralai/mistral-large-2512": (2, 10),
    20	    # Fallback models (in ALLOWED_MODELS)
    21	    "qwen/qwen3-30b-a3b": (1, 5),
    22	    "mistralai/mistral-medium-3": (2, 10),
    23	    "openai/gpt-5.2": (3, 15),
    24	    # Legacy rates (kept for historical UsageRecord cost lookups)
    25	    "x-ai/grok-4.1-fast": (1, 5),
    26	    "minimax/minimax-m2.1": (1, 5),
    27	    "moonshotai/kimi-k2.5": (1, 5),
    28	    "google/gemini-3-flash-preview": (1, 5),
    29	    "google/gemini-3-pro-preview": (3, 15),
    30	    "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5": (3, 15),
    31	    "anthropic/claude-opus-4.6": (15, 75),
    32	}
    33	DEFAULT_RATE = (3, 15)
    34	MIN_CREDITS_FOR_CHAT = 10
    35	
    36	# Estimated cost per mode for pre-debit (generous upper bound to avoid under-debit).
    37	# Internal mode IDs are kept for compatibility: quick=Flash, balanced=Pro.
    38	MODE_ESTIMATED_COST: dict[str, int] = {"quick": 5, "balanced": 15}
    39	
    40	
    41	def get_estimated_cost(mode: str) -> int:
    42	    """Return estimated credit cost for a mode (used for pre-debit)."""
    43	    return MODE_ESTIMATED_COST.get(mode, MODE_ESTIMATED_COST["balanced"])
    44	
    45	
    46	def calculate_cost(prompt_tokens: int, completion_tokens: int, model: str, mode: str | None = None) -> int:
    47	    """Calculate credit cost for token usage, with optional mode multiplier."""
    48	    input_rate, output_rate = CREDIT_RATES.get(model, DEFAULT_RATE)
    49	    input_cost = round(prompt_tokens * input_rate / 1000)
    50	    output_cost = round(completion_tokens * output_rate / 1000)
    51	    base_cost = max(1, input_cost + output_cost)
    52	    # Apply mode multiplier
    53	    multiplier = settings.MODE_CREDIT_MULTIPLIER.get(mode or "balanced", 1.0)
    54	    return max(1, int(base_cost * multiplier))
    55	
    56	
    57	async def get_user_credits(db: AsyncSession, user_id: UUID) -> int:
    58	    """Get user's current credit balance."""
    59	    user = await db.get(User, user_id)
    60	    return user.credits_balance if user else 0
    61	
    62	
    63	async def debit_credits(
    64	    db: AsyncSession,
    65	    user_id: UUID,
    66	    cost: int,
    67	    reason: str,
    68	    ref_type: Optional[str] = None,
    69	    ref_id: Optional[str] = None,
    70	) -> Optional[UUID]:
    71	    """Atomically debit credits. Returns the CreditLedger entry ID on success, None if insufficient.
    72	
    73	    The balance update and ledger entry are written in the same transaction.
    74	    Caller must call db.commit() to persist changes.
    75	    """
    76	    if cost <= 0:
    77	        raise ValueError("Cost must be positive")
    78	
    79	    result = await db.execute(
    80	        sa.update(User)
    81	        .where(User.id == user_id)
    82	        .where(User.credits_balance >= cost)
    83	        .values(credits_balance=User.credits_balance - cost)
    84	        .returning(User.credits_balance)
    85	    )
    86	    row = result.fetchone()
    87	
    88	    if row is None:
    89	        return None
    90	
    91	    new_balance = row[0]
    92	    ledger = CreditLedger(
    93	        user_id=user_id,
    94	        delta=-cost,
    95	        balance_after=new_balance,
    96	        reason=reason,
    97	        ref_type=ref_type,
    98	        ref_id=ref_id,
    99	    )
   100	    db.add(ledger)
   101	    # Flush to ensure ledger is written in same transaction as balance update
   102	    await db.flush()
   103	    return ledger.id
   104	
   105	
   106	async def credit_credits(
   107	    db: AsyncSession,
   108	    user_id: UUID,
   109	    amount: int,
   110	    reason: str,
   111	    ref_type: Optional[str] = None,
   112	    ref_id: Optional[str] = None,
   113	) -> int:
   114	    """Add credits. Returns new balance.
   115	
   116	    The balance update and ledger entry are written in the same transaction.
   117	    Caller must call db.commit() to persist changes.
   118	    """
   119	    if amount <= 0:
   120	        raise ValueError("Amount must be positive")
   121	
   122	    result = await db.execute(
   123	        sa.update(User)
   124	        .where(User.id == user_id)
   125	        .values(credits_balance=User.credits_balance + amount)
   126	        .returning(User.credits_balance)
   127	    )
   128	    new_balance = result.scalar_one()
   129	
   130	    ledger = CreditLedger(
   131	        user_id=user_id,
   132	        delta=amount,
   133	        balance_after=new_balance,
   134	        reason=reason,
   135	        ref_type=ref_type,
   136	        ref_id=ref_id,
   137	    )
   138	    db.add(ledger)
   139	    # Flush to ensure ledger is written in same transaction as balance update
   140	    await db.flush()
   141	    return new_balance
   142	
   143	
   144	async def record_usage(
   145	    db: AsyncSession,
   146	    user_id: UUID,
   147	    message_id: Optional[UUID],
   148	    model: str,
   149	    prompt_tokens: int,
   150	    completion_tokens: int,
   151	    cost_credits: int,
   152	) -> UsageRecord:
   153	    """Record detailed usage information."""
   154	    usage = UsageRecord(
   155	        user_id=user_id,
   156	        message_id=message_id,
   157	        model=model,
   158	        prompt_tokens=prompt_tokens,
   159	        completion_tokens=completion_tokens,
   160	        total_tokens=prompt_tokens + completion_tokens,
   161	        cost_credits=cost_credits,
   162	    )
   163	    db.add(usage)
   164	    return usage
   165	
   166	
   167	async def reconcile_credits(
   168	    db: AsyncSession,
   169	    user_id: UUID,
   170	    predebit_ledger_id: UUID,
   171	    pre_debited: int,
   172	    actual_cost: int,
   173	) -> None:
   174	    """Reconcile pre-debited credits against actual cost after streaming.
   175	
   176	    Updates the ORIGINAL ledger entry in-place so each chat produces exactly
   177	    one ledger row (reason="chat") instead of two (predebit + reconcile).
   178	
   179	    - If pre_debited == actual_cost → no-op
   180	    - If diff != 0 → adjust user balance and update the original ledger entry
   181	    """
   182	    diff = pre_debited - actual_cost
   183	    if diff == 0:
   184	        return
   185	
   186	    balance_result = await db.execute(
   187	        sa.update(User)
   188	        .where(User.id == user_id)
   189	        .values(credits_balance=User.credits_balance + diff)
   190	        .returning(User.credits_balance)
   191	    )
   192	    if balance_result.scalar_one_or_none() is None:
   193	        raise RuntimeError(f"User {user_id} not found during credit reconciliation")
   194	
   195	    # Update the original ledger entry to reflect actual cost
   196	    ledger_result = await db.execute(
   197	        sa.update(CreditLedger)
   198	        .where(CreditLedger.id == predebit_ledger_id)
   199	        .values(
   200	            delta=-actual_cost,
   201	            balance_after=CreditLedger.balance_after + diff,
   202	        )
   203	        .returning(CreditLedger.id)
   204	    )
   205	    if ledger_result.scalar_one_or_none() is None:
   206	        raise RuntimeError(
   207	            f"Predebit ledger {predebit_ledger_id} not found during credit reconciliation"
   208	        )
   209	    await db.flush()
   210	
   211	
   212	async def ensure_monthly_credits(db: AsyncSession, user: User) -> None:
   213	    """Grant monthly credits if last grant was over 30 days ago.
   214	
   215	    Idempotency: checks CreditLedger for any recent 'monthly_allowance' within 30 days.
   216	    """
   217	    from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
   218	
   219	    if (user.plan or "free").lower() != "free":
   220	        return
   221	
   222	    # Determine if grant needed based on timestamp
   223	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   224	    last = user.monthly_credits_granted_at
   225	    if last is not None and last.tzinfo is None:
   226	        # Treat naive as UTC
   227	        last = last.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
   228	    needs_grant = last is None or (now - last) >= timedelta(days=30)
   229	    if not needs_grant:
   230	        return
   231	
   232	    # Check ledger for idempotency within last 30 days
   233	    cutoff = now - timedelta(days=30)
   234	    existing = await db.scalar(
   235	        sa.select(CreditLedger)
   236	        .where(CreditLedger.user_id == user.id)
   237	        .where(CreditLedger.reason == "monthly_allowance")
   238	        .where(CreditLedger.created_at >= cutoff)
   239	    )
   240	    if existing:
   241	        # Still update marker to avoid repeatedly checking in future requests
   242	        user.monthly_credits_granted_at = now
   243	        await db.flush()
   244	        return
   245	
   246	    # Only free users are eligible in this path.
   247	    allowance = int(settings.PLAN_FREE_MONTHLY_CREDITS or 0)
   248	
   249	    if allowance <= 0:
   250	        # Nothing to grant
   251	        user.monthly_credits_granted_at = now
   252	        await db.flush()
   253	        return
   254	
   255	    # Grant credits and update marker
   256	    await credit_credits(
   257	        db,
   258	        user_id=user.id,
   259	        amount=allowance,
   260	        reason="monthly_allowance",
   261	        ref_type="monthly_cycle",
   262	        ref_id=f"monthly_{now.year}_{now.month}",
   263	    )
   264	    user.monthly_credits_granted_at = now
   265	    await db.flush()

     9	from markupsafe import escape as html_escape
    10	
    11	_MAX_MESSAGES = 500
    12	
    13	# Codepoints forbidden by XML 1.0 Char production (https://www.w3.org/TR/xml/#charsets):
    14	#   C0 controls (except \t\n\r): \x00-\x08, \x0B, \x0C, \x0E-\x1F
    15	#   Unpaired surrogates:         \uD800-\uDFFF
    16	#   Non-characters:              \uFFFE, \uFFFF
    17	# python-docx rejects these with ValueError; weasyprint+lxml can misbehave similarly.
    18	_XML_INVALID_RE = re.compile(
    19	    r"[\x00-\x08\x0B\x0C\x0E-\x1F\uD800-\uDFFF\uFFFE\uFFFF]"
    20	)
    21	
    22	
    23	def _sanitize_xml_text(s: Any) -> str:
    24	    """Coerce to str and strip XML-incompatible codepoints.
    25	
    26	    None/non-str inputs fall back to '' / str(s) as a last-line defense —
    27	    upstream callers (export.py) are expected to default None already.
    28	    """
    29	    if s is None:
    30	        return ""
    31	    return _XML_INVALID_RE.sub("", str(s))
    32	
    33	
    34	def _extract_citations(messages: List[Any]) -> list[dict]:
    35	    """Collect unique citations across all messages."""
    36	    seen: set[int] = set()
    37	    citations: list[dict] = []
    38	    for msg in messages:
    39	        raw = msg.citations
    40	        if not raw or not isinstance(raw, list):
    41	            continue
    42	        for c in raw:
    43	            if not isinstance(c, dict):
    44	                continue
    45	            idx = c.get("ref_index", 0)
    46	            if idx not in seen:
    47	                seen.add(idx)
    48	                citations.append(c)
    49	    return sorted(citations, key=lambda c: c.get("ref_index", 0))
    50	
    51	
    52	def _format_footnote(c: dict) -> str:
    53	    page = c.get("page", "?")
    54	    doc = c.get("document_filename", "")
    55	    snippet = c.get("text_snippet", "")[:80]
    56	    source = f"{doc}, " if doc else ""
    57	    return f"Page {page}, {source}\"{snippet}...\""
    58	
    59	
    60	def render_markdown(title: str, doc_name: str, messages: List[Any]) -> str:
    61	    if len(messages) > _MAX_MESSAGES:
    62	        raise ValueError(f"Export limited to {_MAX_MESSAGES} messages")
    63	
    64	    lines = [
    65	        f"# {title}",
    66	        f"*Document: {doc_name}*",
    67	        f"*Exported: {datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC')}*",
    68	        "",
    69	        "---",
    70	        "",
    71	    ]
    72	
    73	    for msg in messages:
    74	        if msg.role == "user":
    75	            lines.append(f"**Q:** {msg.content}")
    76	        else:
    77	            text = re.sub(r"\[(\d+)\]", r"[^\1]", msg.content)
    78	            lines.append(f"**A:** {text}")
    79	        lines.append("")
    80	
    81	    citations = _extract_citations(messages)
    82	    if citations:
    83	        lines.append("---")
    84	        lines.append("## References")
    85	        lines.append("")
    86	        for c in citations:
    87	            idx = c.get("ref_index", 0)
    88	            lines.append(f"[^{idx}]: {_format_footnote(c)}")
    89	
    90	    lines.extend(["", "---", "*Generated by DocTalk — www.doctalk.site*"])
    91	    return "\n".join(lines)
    92	
    93	
    94	class ExportError(Exception):
    95	    """Raised when export rendering fails."""
    96	
    97	
    98	def render_docx(title: str, doc_name: str, messages: List[Any]) -> io.BytesIO:
    99	    if len(messages) > _MAX_MESSAGES:
   100	        raise ValueError(f"Export limited to {_MAX_MESSAGES} messages")
   101	
   102	    try:
   103	        from docx import Document
   104	    except ImportError:
   105	        raise ExportError("DOCX export is not available: python-docx not installed")
   106	    from docx.shared import Pt, RGBColor
   107	
   108	    safe_title = _sanitize_xml_text(title)
   109	    safe_doc_name = _sanitize_xml_text(doc_name)
   110	
   111	    doc = Document()
   112	    doc.add_heading(safe_title, level=1)
   113	    p = doc.add_paragraph()
   114	    run = p.add_run(
   115	        f"Document: {safe_doc_name} | Exported: {datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC')}"
   116	    )
   117	    run.font.size = Pt(9)
   118	    run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor(128, 128, 128)
   119	
   120	    for msg in messages:
   121	        content = _sanitize_xml_text(msg.content)
   122	        if msg.role == "user":
   123	            p = doc.add_paragraph()
   124	            run = p.add_run(f"Q: {content}")
   125	            run.bold = True
   126	        else:
   127	            doc.add_paragraph(content)
   128	
   129	    citations = _extract_citations(messages)
   130	    if citations:
   131	        doc.add_heading("References", level=2)
   132	        for c in citations:
   133	            idx = c.get("ref_index", 0)
   134	            doc.add_paragraph(f"[{idx}] {_sanitize_xml_text(_format_footnote(c))}", style="List Number")
   135	
   136	    p = doc.add_paragraph()
   137	    run = p.add_run("Generated by DocTalk — www.doctalk.site")
   138	    run.font.size = Pt(8)
   139	    run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor(160, 160, 160)
   140	
   141	    try:
   142	        buf = io.BytesIO()
   143	        doc.save(buf)
   144	        buf.seek(0)
   145	        return buf
   146	    except Exception as e:
   147	        raise ExportError(f"DOCX rendering failed: {e}") from e
   148	
   149	
   150	def render_pdf(title: str, doc_name: str, messages: List[Any]) -> io.BytesIO:
   151	    if len(messages) > _MAX_MESSAGES:
   152	        raise ValueError(f"Export limited to {_MAX_MESSAGES} messages")
   153	
   154	    try:
   155	        from weasyprint import HTML
   156	    except (ImportError, OSError):
   157	        raise ExportError("PDF export is not available: weasyprint not installed or system libraries missing")
   158	
   159	    safe_title = html_escape(_sanitize_xml_text(title))
   160	    safe_doc = html_escape(_sanitize_xml_text(doc_name))
   161	    date_str = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
   162	
   163	    msg_html = []
   164	    for msg in messages:
   165	        safe_content = html_escape(_sanitize_xml_text(msg.content))
   166	        if msg.role == "user":
   167	            msg_html.append(f'<div class="q"><strong>Q:</strong> {safe_content}</div>')
   168	        else:
   169	            msg_html.append(f'<div class="a">{safe_content}</div>')
   170	
   171	    citations = _extract_citations(messages)
   172	    refs_html = ""
   173	    if citations:
   174	        refs_items = []
   175	        for c in citations:
   176	            idx = c.get("ref_index", 0)
   177	            refs_items.append(f"<li>[{idx}] {html_escape(_sanitize_xml_text(_format_footnote(c)))}</li>")
   178	        refs_html = f'<h2>References</h2><ol>{"".join(refs_items)}</ol>'
   179	
   180	    html_str = f"""<!DOCTYPE html>
   181	<html><head><meta charset="utf-8">
   182	<style>
   183	  body {{ font-family: 'Noto Sans CJK SC', 'Noto Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 11pt; color: #333; margin: 2cm; }}
   184	  h1 {{ font-size: 18pt; margin-bottom: 4pt; }}
   185	  .meta {{ color: #888; font-size: 9pt; margin-bottom: 16pt; }}
   186	  .q {{ background: #f5f5f5; padding: 8pt; margin: 6pt 0; border-radius: 4pt; }}
   187	  .a {{ padding: 8pt; margin: 6pt 0; }}
   188	  h2 {{ font-size: 13pt; margin-top: 20pt; }}
   189	  ol {{ font-size: 9pt; color: #666; }}
   190	  .footer {{ text-align: center; color: #aaa; font-size: 8pt; margin-top: 30pt; }}
   191	</style></head><body>
   192	<h1>{safe_title}</h1>
   193	<div class="meta">Document: {safe_doc} | Exported: {date_str}</div>
   194	{"".join(msg_html)}
   195	{refs_html}
   196	<div class="footer">Generated by DocTalk &mdash; www.doctalk.site</div>
   197	</body></html>"""
   198	
   199	    try:
   200	        buf = io.BytesIO()
   201	        HTML(string=html_str).write_pdf(buf)
   202	        buf.seek(0)
   203	        return buf
   204	    except Exception as e:
   205	        raise ExportError(f"PDF rendering failed: {e}") from e

    70	def _as_utc(dt):
    71	    if dt is None:
    72	        return None
    73	    if dt.tzinfo is None:
    74	        return dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    87	async def _verify_document(document_id: uuid.UUID, user: User, db: AsyncSession) -> Document | None:

    91	    return doc

    94	async def _enforce_free_extraction_limit(user: User, db: AsyncSession) -> bool:
    95	    if (user.plan or "free").lower() != "free":
    96	        return True
    97	    window_start = _as_utc(user.monthly_credits_granted_at)
    98	    if window_start is None:
    99	        window_start = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=30)
   100	    used = await db.scalar(
   101	        select(func.count())
   102	        .select_from(DocumentJob)
   103	        .where(DocumentJob.user_id == user.id)
   104	        .where(DocumentJob.job_type == EXTRACTION_JOB_TYPE)
   105	        .where(DocumentJob.status.in_(["queued", "running", "succeeded"]))
   106	        .where(DocumentJob.created_at >= window_start)
   107	    )
   108	    return int(used or 0) < FREE_MONTHLY_EXTRACTION_LIMIT

   111	async def _queue_extraction(
   112	    *,
   113	    user: User,

   117	    locale: str | None,

   122	        template = get_template(template_key)
   123	    except ValueError:
   124	        template = get_template("executive_summary")
   125

   186	    db.add(
