backend/scripts/benchmark_results/chunks_injection_from_existing_2026-05-04.json:29:      "text": "Germany, India, Israel, and Taiwan for fiscal years 2005 through 2023.Note 15 - Shareholders’ EquityCapital Return ProgramIn August 2023, our Board of Directors approved an increase to our share repurchase program of an additional $25.0 billion, without expiration.During fiscalyear 2024, we repurchased 21 million shares of our common stock for $9.7 billion.As of January 28, 2024, we were authorized, subject to certain specifications,to repurchase additional shares of our common stock up to $22.5 billion.From January 29, 2024 through February 16, 2024, we repurchased 2.8 million sharesfor $1.9 billion pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan.Our share repurchase program aims to offset dilution from shares issued to employees.We may pursueadditional share repurchases as we weigh market factors and other investment opportunities.During fiscal years 2024, 2023, and 2022, we paid $395 million, $398 million, and $399 million in cash dividends to our shareholders, respectively.Our cashdividend program and the payment of future cash dividends under that program are subject to our Board of Directors' continuing determination that the dividendprogram and the declaration of dividends thereunder are in the best interests of our shareholders.In fiscal year 2022, we retired our existing 349 million treasury shares.These shares assumed the status of authorized and unissued shares upon retirement.The excess of repurchase price over par value was allocated between additional paid-in capital and retained earnings, resulting in a reduction in additional paidin capital by $20 million and retained earnings by $12.0 billion.Any future repurchased shares will assume the status of authorized and unissued shares.77NVIDIA Corporation and SubsidiariesNotes to the Consolidated Financial Statements(Continued)Note 16 - Employee Retirement PlansWe provide tax-qualified defined contribution plans to eligible employees in the U.S.and certain other countries.Our contribution expense for fiscal years 2024,2023, and 2022 was $255 million, $227 million, and $168 million, respectively.Note 17 - Segment InformationOur Chief Executive Officer, who is considered to be our chief operating decision maker, or CODM, reviews financial information presented on an operatingsegment basis for purposes of making decisions and assessing financial performance.The Compute & Networking segment includes our Data Center accelerated computing platform; networking; automotive artificial intelligence, or AI, Cockpit,autonomous driving development agreements, and autonomous vehicle solutions; electric vehicle computing platforms; Jetson for robotics and other embeddedplatforms; NVIDIA AI Enterprise and other software; and DGX Cloud.The Graphics segment includes GeForce GPUs for gaming and PCs, the GeForce NOW game streaming service and related infrastructure, and solutions forgaming platforms; Quadro/NVIDIA RTX GPUs for enterprise workstation graphics; virtual GPU software for cloud-based visual and virtual computing; automotiveplatforms for infotainment systems; and Omniverse Enterprise software for building and operating 3D internet applications.Operating results by segment include costs or expenses that are directly attributable to each segment, and costs or expenses that are leveraged across our",

Employee Retirement PlansWe provide tax-qualified defined contribution plans to eligible employees in the U.S.and certain other countries.Our contribution expense for fiscal years 2024,2023, and 2022 was $255 million, $227 million, and $168 million, respectively.Note 17 - backend/scripts/benchmark_results/chunks_injection_from_existing_2026-05-04.json:403:      "text": "Germany, India, Israel, and Taiwan for fiscal years 2005 through 2023.Note 15 - Shareholders’ EquityCapital Return ProgramIn August 2023, our Board of Directors approved an increase to our share repurchase program of an additional $25.0 billion, without expiration.During fiscalyear 2024, we repurchased 21 million shares of our common stock for $9.7 billion.As of January 28, 2024, we were authorized, subject to certain specifications,to repurchase additional shares of our common stock up to $22.5 billion.From January 29, 2024 through February 16, 2024, we repurchased 2.8 million sharesfor $1.9 billion pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan.Our share repurchase program aims to offset dilution from shares issued to employees.We may pursueadditional share repurchases as we weigh market factors and other investment opportunities.During fiscal years 2024, 2023, and 2022, we paid $395 million, $398 million, and $399 million in cash dividends to our shareholders, respectively.Our cashdividend program and the payment of future cash dividends under that program are subject to our Board of Directors' continuing determination that the dividendprogram and the declaration of dividends thereunder are in the best interests of our shareholders.In fiscal year 2022, we retired our existing 349 million treasury shares.These shares assumed the status of authorized and unissued shares upon retirement.The excess of repurchase price over par value was allocated between additional paid-in capital and retained earnings, resulting in a reduction in additional paidin capital by $20 million and retained earnings by $12.0 billion.Any future repurchased shares will assume the status of authorized and unissued shares.77NVIDIA Corporation and SubsidiariesNotes to the Consolidated Financial Statements(Continued)Note 16 - Employee Retirement PlansWe provide tax-qualified defined contribution plans to eligible employees in the U.S.and certain other countries.Our contribution expense for fiscal years 2024,2023, and 2022 was $255 million, $227 million, and $168 million, respectively.Note 17 - Segment InformationOur Chief Executive Officer, who is considered to be our chief operating decision maker, or CODM, reviews financial information presented on an operatingsegment basis for purposes of making decisions and assessing financial performance.The Compute & Networking segment includes our Data Center accelerated computing platform; networking; automotive artificial intelligence, or AI, Cockpit,autonomous driving development agreements, and autonomous vehicle solutions; electric vehicle computing platforms; Jetson for robotics and other embeddedplatforms; NVIDIA AI Enterprise and other software; and DGX Cloud.The Graphics segment includes GeForce GPUs for gaming and PCs, the GeForce NOW game streaming service and related infrastructure, and solutions forgaming platforms; Quadro/NVIDIA RTX GPUs for enterprise workstation graphics; virtual GPU software for cloud-based visual and virtual computing; automotiveplatforms for infotainment systems; and Omniverse Enterprise software for building and operating 3D internet applications.Operating results by segment include costs or expenses that are directly attributable to each segment, and costs or expenses that are leveraged across our",
backend/scripts/benchmark_results/chunks_injection_from_existing_2026-05-04.json:461:      "text": "Germany, India, Israel, and Taiwan for fiscal years 2005 through 2023.Note 15 - Shareholders’ EquityCapital Return ProgramIn August 2023, our Board of Directors approved an increase to our share repurchase program of an additional $25.0 billion, without expiration.During fiscalyear 2024, we repurchased 21 million shares of our common stock for $9.7 billion.As of January 28, 2024, we were authorized, subject to certain specifications,to repurchase additional shares of our common stock up to $22.5 billion.From January 29, 2024 through February 16, 2024, we repurchased 2.8 million sharesfor $1.9 billion pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan.Our share repurchase program aims to offset dilution from shares issued to employees.We may pursueadditional share repurchases as we weigh market factors and other investment opportunities.During fiscal years 2024, 2023, and 2022, we paid $395 million, $398 million, and $399 million in cash dividends to our shareholders, respectively.Our cashdividend program and the payment of future cash dividends under that program are subject to our Board of Directors' continuing determination that the dividendprogram and the declaration of dividends thereunder are in the best interests of our shareholders.In fiscal year 2022, we retired our existing 349 million treasury shares.These shares assumed the status of authorized and unissued shares upon retirement.The excess of repurchase price over par value was allocated between additional paid-in capital and retained earnings, resulting in a reduction in additional paidin capital by $20 million and retained earnings by $12.0 billion.Any future repurchased shares will assume the status of authorized and unissued shares.77NVIDIA Corporation and SubsidiariesNotes to the Consolidated Financial Statements(Continued)Note 16 - Employee Retirement PlansWe provide tax-qualified defined contribution plans to eligible employees in the U.S.and certain other countries.Our contribution expense for fiscal years 2024,2023, and 2022 was $255 million, $227 million, and $168 million, respectively.Note 17 - Segment InformationOur Chief Executive Officer, who is considered to be our chief operating decision maker, or CODM, reviews financial information presented on an operatingsegment basis for purposes of making decisions and assessing financial performance.The Compute & Networking segment includes our Data Center accelerated computing platform; networking; automotive artificial intelligence, or AI, Cockpit,autonomous driving development agreements, and autonomous vehicle solutions; electric vehicle computing platforms; Jetson for robotics and other embeddedplatforms; NVIDIA AI Enterprise and other software; and DGX Cloud.The Graphics segment includes GeForce GPUs for gaming and PCs, the GeForce NOW game streaming service and related infrastructure, and solutions forgaming platforms; Quadro/NVIDIA RTX GPUs for enterprise workstation graphics; virtual GPU software for cloud-based visual and virtual computing; automotiveplatforms for infotainment systems; and Omniverse Enterprise software for building and operating 3D internet applications.Operating results by segment include costs or expenses that are directly attributable to each segment, and costs or expenses that are leveraged across our",
backend/scripts/benchmark_results/chunks_injection_from_existing_2026-05-04.json:827:      "text": "TOP500 list, including 24 of the top 30 systems on the Green500 list.Gamers choose NVIDIA GPUs to enjoy immersive, increasingly cinematic virtual worlds.In addition to serving the growing number of gamers, the market for PCGPUs is expanding because of the burgeoning population of live streamers, broadcasters, artists, and creators.With the advent of generative AI, we expect abroader set of PC users to choose NVIDIA GPUs for running generative AI applications locally on their PC, which is critical for privacy, latency, and cost-sensitiveAI applications.Professional artists, architects and designers use NVIDIA partner products accelerated with our GPUs and software platform for a range of creative and designuse cases, such as creating visual effects in movies or designing buildings and products.In addition, generative AI is expanding the market for our workstationclass GPUs, as more enterprise customers develop and deploy AI applications with their data on-premises.Headquartered in Santa Clara, California, NVIDIA was incorporated in California in April 1993 and reincorporated in Delaware in April 1998.Our BusinessesWe report our business results in two segments.The Compute & Networking segment is comprised of our Data Center accelerated computing platforms and end-to-end networking platforms including Quantumfor InfiniBand and Spectrum for Ethernet; our NVIDIA DRIVE automated-driving platform and automotive development agreements; Jetson robotics and otherembedded platforms; NVIDIA AI Enterprise and other software; and DGX Cloud software and services.The Graphics segment includes GeForce GPUs for gaming and PCs, the GeForce NOW game streaming service and related infrastructure; Quadro/NVIDIARTX GPUs for enterprise workstation graphics; virtual GPU, or vGPU, software for cloud-based visual and virtual computing; automotive platforms forinfotainment systems; and Omniverse Enterprise software for building and operating metaverse and 3D internet applications.Our MarketsWe specialize in markets where our computing platforms can provide tremendous acceleration for applications.These platforms incorporate processors,interconnects, software, algorithms, systems, and services to deliver unique value.Our platforms address four large markets where our expertise is critical: DataCenter, Gaming, Professional Visualization, and Automotive.Data CenterThe NVIDIA Data Center platform is focused on accelerating the most compute-intensive workloads, such as AI, data analytics, graphics and scientificcomputing, delivering significantly better performance and power efficiency relative to conventional CPU-only approaches.It is deployed in cloud, hyperscale,on-premises and edge data centers.The platform consists of compute and networking offerings typically delivered to customers as systems, subsystems, ormodules, along with software and services.Our compute offerings include supercomputing platforms and servers, bringing together our energy efficient GPUs, DPUs, interconnects, and fully optimized AIand high-performance computing, or HPC, software stacks.In addition, they include NVIDIA AI Enterprise software; our DGX Cloud service; and a growing bodyof acceleration libraries, APIs, SDKs, and domain-specific application frameworks.Our networking offerings include end-to-end platforms for InfiniBand and Ethernet, consisting of network adapters, cables, DPUs, and switch systems, as well asa full software stack.",
backend/scripts/benchmark_results/chunks_injection_from_existing_2026-05-04.json:843:      "text": "Germany, India, Israel, and Taiwan for fiscal years 2005 through 2023.Note 15 - Shareholders’ EquityCapital Return ProgramIn August 2023, our Board of Directors approved an increase to our share repurchase program of an additional $25.0 billion, without expiration.During fiscalyear 2024, we repurchased 21 million shares of our common stock for $9.7 billion.As of January 28, 2024, we were authorized, subject to certain specifications,to repurchase additional shares of our common stock up to $22.5 billion.From January 29, 2024 through February 16, 2024, we repurchased 2.8 million sharesfor $1.9 billion pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan.Our share repurchase program aims to offset dilution from shares issued to employees.We may pursueadditional share repurchases as we weigh market factors and other investment opportunities.During fiscal years 2024, 2023, and 2022, we paid $395 million, $398 million, and $399 million in cash dividends to our shareholders, respectively.Our cashdividend program and the payment of future cash dividends under that program are subject to our Board of Directors' continuing determination that the dividendprogram and the declaration of dividends thereunder are in the best interests of our shareholders.In fiscal year 2022, we retired our existing 349 million treasury shares.These shares assumed the status of authorized and unissued shares upon retirement.The excess of repurchase price over par value was allocated between additional paid-in capital and retained earnings, resulting in a reduction in additional paidin capital by $20 million and retained earnings by $12.0 billion.Any future repurchased shares will assume the status of authorized and unissued shares.77NVIDIA Corporation and SubsidiariesNotes to the Consolidated Financial Statements(Continued)Note 16 - Employee Retirement PlansWe provide tax-qualified defined contribution plans to eligible employees in the U.S.and certain other countries.Our contribution expense for fiscal years 2024,2023, and 2022 was $255 million, $227 million, and $168 million, respectively.Note 17 - Segment InformationOur Chief Executive Officer, who is considered to be our chief operating decision maker, or CODM, reviews financial information presented on an operatingsegment basis for purposes of making decisions and assessing financial performance.The Compute & Networking segment includes our Data Center accelerated computing platform; networking; automotive artificial intelligence, or AI, Cockpit,autonomous driving development agreements, and autonomous vehicle solutions; electric vehicle computing platforms; Jetson for robotics and other embeddedplatforms; NVIDIA AI Enterprise and other software; and DGX Cloud.The Graphics segment includes GeForce GPUs for gaming and PCs, the GeForce NOW game streaming service and related infrastructure, and solutions forgaming platforms; Quadro/NVIDIA RTX GPUs for enterprise workstation graphics; virtual GPU software for cloud-based visual and virtual computing; automotiveplatforms for infotainment systems; and Omniverse Enterprise software for building and operating 3D internet applications.Operating results by segment include costs or expenses that are directly attributable to each segment, and costs or expenses that are leveraged across our",
backend/scripts/benchmark_results/chunks_injection_from_existing_2026-05-04.json:901:      "text": "Germany, India, Israel, and Taiwan for fiscal years 2005 through 2023.Note 15 - Shareholders’ EquityCapital Return ProgramIn August 2023, our Board of Directors approved an increase to our share repurchase program of an additional $25.0 billion, without expiration.During fiscalyear 2024, we repurchased 21 million shares of our common stock for $9.7 billion.As of January 28, 2024, we were authorized, subject to certain specifications,to repurchase additional shares of our common stock up to $22.5 billion.From January 29, 2024 through February 16, 2024, we repurchased 2.8 million sharesfor $1.9 billion pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan.Our share repurchase program aims to offset dilution from shares issued to employees.We may pursueadditional share repurchases as we weigh market factors and other investment opportunities.During fiscal years 2024, 2023, and 2022, we paid $395 million, $398 million, and $399 million in cash dividends to our shareholders, respectively.Our cashdividend program and the payment of future cash dividends under that program are subject to our Board of Directors' continuing determination that the dividendprogram and the declaration of dividends thereunder are in the best interests of our shareholders.In fiscal year 2022, we retired our existing 349 million treasury shares.These shares assumed the status of authorized and unissued shares upon retirement.The excess of repurchase price over par value was allocated between additional paid-in capital and retained earnings, resulting in a reduction in additional paidin capital by $20 million and retained earnings by $12.0 billion.Any future repurchased shares will assume the status of authorized and unissued shares.77NVIDIA Corporation and SubsidiariesNotes to the Consolidated Financial Statements(Continued)Note 16 - Employee Retirement PlansWe provide tax-qualified defined contribution plans to eligible employees in the U.S.and certain other countries.Our contribution expense for fiscal years 2024,2023, and 2022 was $255 million, $227 million, and $168 million, respectively.Note 17 - Segment InformationOur Chief Executive Officer, who is considered to be our chief operating decision maker, or CODM, reviews financial information presented on an operatingsegment basis for purposes of making decisions and assessing financial performance.The Compute & Networking segment includes our Data Center accelerated computing platform; networking; automotive artificial intelligence, or AI, Cockpit,autonomous driving development agreements, and autonomous vehicle solutions; electric vehicle computing platforms; Jetson for robotics and other embeddedplatforms; NVIDIA AI Enterprise and other software; and DGX Cloud.The Graphics segment includes GeForce GPUs for gaming and PCs, the GeForce NOW game streaming service and related infrastructure, and solutions forgaming platforms; Quadro/NVIDIA RTX GPUs for enterprise workstation graphics; virtual GPU software for cloud-based visual and virtual computing; automotiveplatforms for infotainment systems; and Omniverse Enterprise software for building and operating 3D internet applications.Operating results by segment include costs or expenses that are directly attributable to each segment, and costs or expenses that are leveraged across our",
backend/scripts/benchmark_results/chunks_injection_from_existing_2026-05-04.json:1127:      "text": "Germany, India, Israel, and Taiwan for fiscal years 2005 through 2023.Note 15 - Shareholders’ EquityCapital Return ProgramIn August 2023, our Board of Directors approved an increase to our share repurchase program of an additional $25.0 billion, without expiration.During fiscalyear 2024, we repurchased 21 million shares of our common stock for $9.7 billion.As of January 28, 2024, we were authorized, subject to certain specifications,to repurchase additional shares of our common stock up to $22.5 billion.From January 29, 2024 through February 16, 2024, we repurchased 2.8 million sharesfor $1.9 billion pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan.Our share repurchase program aims to offset dilution from shares issued to employees.We may pursueadditional share repurchases as we weigh market factors and other investment opportunities.During fiscal years 2024, 2023, and 2022, we paid $395 million, $398 million, and $399 million in cash dividends to our shareholders, respectively.Our cashdividend program and the payment of future cash dividends under that program are subject to our Board of Directors' continuing determination that the dividendprogram and the declaration of dividends thereunder are in the best interests of our shareholders.In fiscal year 2022, we retired our existing 349 million treasury shares.These shares assumed the status of authorized and unissued shares upon retirement.The excess of repurchase price over par value was allocated between additional paid-in capital and retained earnings, resulting in a reduction in additional paidin capital by $20 million and retained earnings by $12.0 billion.Any future repurchased shares will assume the status of authorized and unissued shares.77NVIDIA Corporation and SubsidiariesNotes to the Consolidated Financial Statements(Continued)Note 16 - Employee Retirement PlansWe provide tax-qualified defined contribution plans to eligible employees in the U.S.and certain other countries.Our contribution expense for fiscal years 2024,2023, and 2022 was $255 million, $227 million, and $168 million, respectively.Note 17 - Segment InformationOur Chief Executive Officer, who is considered to be our chief operating decision maker, or CODM, reviews financial information presented on an operatingsegment basis for purposes of making decisions and assessing financial performance.The Compute & Networking segment includes our Data Center accelerated computing platform; networking; automotive artificial intelligence, or AI, Cockpit,autonomous driving development agreements, and autonomous vehicle solutions; electric vehicle computing platforms; Jetson for robotics and other embeddedplatforms; NVIDIA AI Enterprise and other software; and DGX Cloud.The Graphics segment includes GeForce GPUs for gaming and PCs, the GeForce NOW game streaming service and related infrastructure, and solutions forgaming platforms; Quadro/NVIDIA RTX GPUs for enterprise workstation graphics; virtual GPU software for cloud-based visual and virtual computing; automotiveplatforms for infotainment systems; and Omniverse Enterprise software for building and operating 3D internet applications.Operating results by segment include costs or expenses that are directly attributable to each segment, and costs or expenses that are leveraged across our",
backend/scripts/benchmark_results/chunks_injection_from_existing_2026-05-04.json:1277:      "text": "TOP500 list, including 24 of the top 30 systems on the Green500 list.Gamers choose NVIDIA GPUs to enjoy immersive, increasingly cinematic virtual worlds.In addition to serving the growing number of gamers, the market for PCGPUs is expanding because of the burgeoning population of live streamers, broadcasters, artists, and creators.With the advent of generative AI, we expect abroader set of PC users to choose NVIDIA GPUs for running generative AI applications locally on their PC, which is critical for privacy, latency, and cost-sensitiveAI applications.Professional artists, architects and designers use NVIDIA partner products accelerated with our GPUs and software platform for a range of creative and designuse cases, such as creating visual effects in movies or designing buildings and products.In addition, generative AI is expanding the market for our workstationclass GPUs, as more enterprise customers develop and deploy AI applications with their data on-premises.Headquartered in Santa Clara, California, NVIDIA was incorporated in California in April 1993 and reincorporated in Delaware in April 1998.Our BusinessesWe report our business results in two segments.The Compute & Networking segment is comprised of our Data Center accelerated computing platforms and end-to-end networking platforms including Quantumfor InfiniBand and Spectrum for Ethernet; our NVIDIA DRIVE automated-driving platform and automotive development agreements; Jetson robotics and otherembedded platforms; NVIDIA AI Enterprise and other software; and DGX Cloud software and services.The Graphics segment includes GeForce GPUs for gaming and PCs, the GeForce NOW game streaming service and related infrastructure; Quadro/NVIDIARTX GPUs for enterprise workstation graphics; virtual GPU, or vGPU, software for cloud-based visual and virtual computing; automotive platforms forinfotainment systems; and Omniverse Enterprise software for building and operating metaverse and 3D internet applications.Our MarketsWe specialize in markets where our computing platforms can provide tremendous acceleration for applications.These platforms incorporate processors,interconnects, software, algorithms, systems, and services to deliver unique value.Our platforms address four large markets where our expertise is critical: DataCenter, Gaming, Professional Visualization, and Automotive.Data CenterThe NVIDIA Data Center platform is focused on accelerating the most compute-intensive workloads, such as AI, data analytics, graphics and scientificcomputing, delivering significantly better performance and power efficiency relative to conventional CPU-only approaches.It is deployed in cloud, hyperscale,on-premises and edge data centers.The platform consists of compute and networking offerings typically delivered to customers as systems, subsystems, ormodules, along with software and services.Our compute offerings include supercomputing platforms and servers, bringing together our energy efficient GPUs, DPUs, interconnects, and fully optimized AIand high-performance computing, or HPC, software stacks.In addition, they include NVIDIA AI Enterprise software; our DGX Cloud service; and a growing bodyof acceleration libraries, APIs, SDKs, and domain-specific application frameworks.Our networking offerings include end-to-end platforms for InfiniBand and Ethernet, consisting of network adapters, cables, DPUs, and switch systems, as well asa full software stack.",
backend/scripts/benchmark_results/chunks_injection_from_existing_2026-05-04.json:1285:      "text": "Germany, India, Israel, and Taiwan for fiscal years 2005 through 2023.Note 15 - Shareholders’ EquityCapital Return ProgramIn August 2023, our Board of Directors approved an increase to our share repurchase program of an additional $25.0 billion, without expiration.During fiscalyear 2024, we repurchased 21 million shares of our common stock for $9.7 billion.As of January 28, 2024, we were authorized, subject to certain specifications,to repurchase additional shares of our common stock up to $22.5 billion.From January 29, 2024 through February 16, 2024, we repurchased 2.8 million sharesfor $1.9 billion pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan.Our share repurchase program aims to offset dilution from shares issued to employees.We may pursueadditional share repurchases as we weigh market factors and other investment opportunities.During fiscal years 2024, 2023, and 2022, we paid $395 million, $398 million, and $399 million in cash dividends to our shareholders, respectively.Our cashdividend program and the payment of future cash dividends under that program are subject to our Board of Directors' continuing determination that the dividendprogram and the declaration of dividends thereunder are in the best interests of our shareholders.In fiscal year 2022, we retired our existing 349 million treasury shares.These shares assumed the status of authorized and unissued shares upon retirement.The excess of repurchase price over par value was allocated between additional paid-in capital and retained earnings, resulting in a reduction in additional paidin capital by $20 million and retained earnings by $12.0 billion.Any future repurchased shares will assume the status of authorized and unissued shares.77NVIDIA Corporation and SubsidiariesNotes to the Consolidated Financial Statements(Continued)Note 16 - Employee Retirement PlansWe provide tax-qualified defined contribution plans to eligible employees in the U.S.and certain other countries.Our contribution expense for fiscal years 2024,2023, and 2022 was $255 million, $227 million, and $168 million, respectively.Note 17 - Segment InformationOur Chief Executive Officer, who is considered to be our chief operating decision maker, or CODM, reviews financial information presented on an operatingsegment basis for purposes of making decisions and assessing financial performance.The Compute & Networking segment includes our Data Center accelerated computing platform; networking; automotive artificial intelligence, or AI, Cockpit,autonomous driving development agreements, and autonomous vehicle solutions; electric vehicle computing platforms; Jetson for robotics and other embeddedplatforms; NVIDIA AI Enterprise and other software; and DGX Cloud.The Graphics segment includes GeForce GPUs for gaming and PCs, the GeForce NOW game streaming service and related infrastructure, and solutions forgaming platforms; Quadro/NVIDIA RTX GPUs for enterprise workstation graphics; virtual GPU software for cloud-based visual and virtual computing; automotiveplatforms for infotainment systems; and Omniverse Enterprise software for building and operating 3D internet applications.Operating results by segment include costs or expenses that are directly attributable to each segment, and costs or expenses that are leveraged across our",

our NVIDIA DRIVE automated-driving platform and automotive development agreements; Jetson robotics and otherembedded platforms; NVIDIA AI Enterprise and other software; and DGX Cloud software and services.The Graphics segment includes GeForce GPUs for gaming and PCs, the GeForce NOW game streaming service and related infrastructure; Quadro/NVIDIARTX GPUs for enterprise workstation graphics; virtual GPU, or vGPU, software for cloud-based visual and virtual computing; automotive platforms forinfotainment systems; and Omniverse Enterprise software for building and operating metaverse and 3D internet applications.Our MarketsWe specialize in markets where our computing platforms can provide tremendous acceleration for applications.These platforms incorporate processors,interconnects, software, algorithms, systems, and services to deliver unique value.Our platforms address four large markets where our expertise is critical: DataCenter, Gaming, Professional Visualization, and Automotive.Data CenterThe NVIDIA Data Center platform is focused on accelerating the most compute-intensive workloads, such as AI, data analytics, graphics and scientificcomputing, delivering significantly better performance and power efficiency relative to conventional CPU-only approaches.It is deployed in cloud, hyperscale,on-premises and edge data centers.The platform consists of compute and networking offerings typically delivered to customers as systems, subsystems, ormodules, along with software and services.Our compute offerings include supercomputing platforms and servers, bringing together our energy efficient GPUs, DPUs, interconnects, and fully optimized AIand high-performance computing, or HPC, software stacks.In addition, they include NVIDIA AI Enterprise software; our DGX Cloud service; and a growing bodyof acceleration libraries, APIs, SDKs, and domain-specific application frameworks.Our networking offerings include end-to-end platforms for InfiniBand and Ethernet, consisting of network adapters, cables, DPUs, and switch systems, as well asa full software stack.",
backend/scripts/benchmark_results/chunks_injection_from_existing_2026-05-04.json:1569:      "text": "Germany, India, Israel, and Taiwan for fiscal years 2005 through 2023.Note 15 - Shareholders’ EquityCapital Return ProgramIn August 2023, our Board of Directors approved an increase to our share repurchase program of an additional $25.0 billion, without expiration.During fiscalyear 2024, we repurchased 21 million shares of our common stock for $9.7 billion.As of January 28, 2024, we were authorized, subject to certain specifications,to repurchase additional shares of our common stock up to $22.5 billion.From January 29, 2024 through February 16, 2024, we repurchased 2.8 million sharesfor $1.9 billion pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan.Our share repurchase program aims to offset dilution from shares issued to employees.We may pursueadditional share repurchases as we weigh market factors and other investment opportunities.During fiscal years 2024, 2023, and 2022, we paid $395 million, $398 million, and $399 million in cash dividends to our shareholders, respectively.Our cashdividend program and the payment of future cash dividends under that program are subject to our Board of Directors' continuing determination that the dividendprogram and the declaration of dividends thereunder are in the best interests of our shareholders.In fiscal year 2022, we retired our existing 349 million treasury shares.These shares assumed the status of authorized and unissued shares upon retirement.The excess of repurchase price over par value was allocated between additional paid-in capital and retained earnings, resulting in a reduction in additional paidin capital by $20 million and retained earnings by $12.0 billion.Any future repurchased shares will assume the status of authorized and unissued shares.77NVIDIA Corporation and SubsidiariesNotes to the Consolidated Financial Statements(Continued)Note 16 - backend/scripts/benchmark_results/chunks_injection_from_existing_2026-05-04.json:2107:      "text": "Germany, India, Israel, and Taiwan for fiscal years 2005 through 2023.Note 15 - Shareholders’ EquityCapital Return ProgramIn August 2023, our Board of Directors approved an increase to our share repurchase program of an additional $25.0 billion, without expiration.During fiscalyear 2024, we repurchased 21 million shares of our common stock for $9.7 billion.As of January 28, 2024, we were authorized, subject to certain specifications,to repurchase additional shares of our common stock up to $22.5 billion.From January 29, 2024 through February 16, 2024, we repurchased 2.8 million sharesfor $1.9 billion pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan.Our share repurchase program aims to offset dilution from shares issued to employees.We may pursueadditional share repurchases as we weigh market factors and other investment opportunities.During fiscal years 2024, 2023, and 2022, we paid $395 million, $398 million, and $399 million in cash dividends to our shareholders, respectively.Our cashdividend program and the payment of future cash dividends under that program are subject to our Board of Directors' continuing determination that the dividendprogram and the declaration of dividends thereunder are in the best interests of our shareholders.In fiscal year 2022, we retired our existing 349 million treasury shares.These shares assumed the status of authorized and unissued shares upon retirement.The excess of repurchase price over par value was allocated between additional paid-in capital and retained earnings, resulting in a reduction in additional paidin capital by $20 million and retained earnings by $12.0 billion.Any future repurchased shares will assume the status of authorized and unissued shares.77NVIDIA Corporation and SubsidiariesNotes to the Consolidated Financial Statements(Continued)Note 16 - Employee Retirement PlansWe provide tax-qualified defined contribution plans to eligible employees in the U.S.and certain other countries.Our contribution expense for fiscal years 2024,2023, and 2022 was $255 million, $227 million, and $168 million, respectively.Note 17 - Segment InformationOur Chief Executive Officer, who is considered to be our chief operating decision maker, or CODM, reviews financial information presented on an operatingsegment basis for purposes of making decisions and assessing financial performance.The Compute & Networking segment includes our Data Center accelerated computing platform; networking; automotive artificial intelligence, or AI, Cockpit,autonomous driving development agreements, and autonomous vehicle solutions; electric vehicle computing platforms; Jetson for robotics and other embeddedplatforms; NVIDIA AI Enterprise and other software; and DGX Cloud.The Graphics segment includes GeForce GPUs for gaming and PCs, the GeForce NOW game streaming service and related infrastructure, and solutions forgaming platforms; Quadro/NVIDIA RTX GPUs for enterprise workstation graphics; virtual GPU software for cloud-based visual and virtual computing; automotiveplatforms for infotainment systems; and Omniverse Enterprise software for building and operating 3D internet applications.Operating results by segment include costs or expenses that are directly attributable to each segment, and costs or expenses that are leveraged across our",

backend/scripts/benchmark_results/chunks_injection_from_existing_2026-05-04.json:2395:      "text": "Germany, India, Israel, and Taiwan for fiscal years 2005 through 2023.Note 15 - Shareholders’ EquityCapital Return ProgramIn August 2023, our Board of Directors approved an increase to our share repurchase program of an additional $25.0 billion, without expiration.During fiscalyear 2024, we repurchased 21 million shares of our common stock for $9.7 billion.As of January 28, 2024, we were authorized, subject to certain specifications,to repurchase additional shares of our common stock up to $22.5 billion.From January 29, 2024 through February 16, 2024, we repurchased 2.8 million sharesfor $1.9 billion pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan.Our share repurchase program aims to offset dilution from shares issued to employees.We may pursueadditional share repurchases as we weigh market factors and other investment opportunities.During fiscal years 2024, 2023, and 2022, we paid $395 million, $398 million, and $399 million in cash dividends to our shareholders, respectively.Our cashdividend program and the payment of future cash dividends under that program are subject to our Board of Directors' continuing determination that the dividendprogram and the declaration of dividends thereunder are in the best interests of our shareholders.In fiscal year 2022, we retired our existing 349 million treasury shares.These shares assumed the status of authorized and unissued shares upon retirement.The excess of repurchase price over par value was allocated between additional paid-in capital and retained earnings, resulting in a reduction in additional paidin capital by $20 million and retained earnings by $12.0 billion.Any future repurchased shares will assume the status of authorized and unissued shares.77NVIDIA Corporation and SubsidiariesNotes to the Consolidated Financial Statements(Continued)Note 16 - Employee Retirement PlansWe provide tax-qualified defined contribution plans to eligible employees in the U.S.and certain other countries.Our contribution expense for fiscal years 2024,2023, and 2022 was $255 million, $227 million, and $168 million, respectively.Note 17 - Segment InformationOur Chief Executive Officer, who is considered to be our chief operating decision maker, or CODM, reviews financial information presented on an operatingsegment basis for purposes of making decisions and assessing financial performance.The Compute & Networking segment includes our Data Center accelerated computing platform; networking; automotive artificial intelligence, or AI, Cockpit,autonomous driving development agreements, and autonomous vehicle solutions; electric vehicle computing platforms; Jetson for robotics and other embeddedplatforms; NVIDIA AI Enterprise and other software; and DGX Cloud.The Graphics segment includes GeForce GPUs for gaming and PCs, the GeForce NOW game streaming service and related infrastructure, and solutions forgaming platforms; Quadro/NVIDIA RTX GPUs for enterprise workstation graphics; virtual GPU software for cloud-based visual and virtual computing; automotiveplatforms for infotainment systems; and Omniverse Enterprise software for building and operating 3D internet applications.Operating results by segment include costs or expenses that are directly attributable to each segment, and costs or expenses that are leveraged across our",
backend/scripts/benchmark_results/chunks_injection_from_existing_2026-05-04.json:2513:      "text": "TOP500 list, including 24 of the top 30 systems on the Green500 list.Gamers choose NVIDIA GPUs to enjoy immersive, increasingly cinematic virtual worlds.In addition to serving the growing number of gamers, the market for PCGPUs is expanding because of the burgeoning population of live streamers, broadcasters, artists, and creators.With the advent of generative AI, we expect abroader set of PC users to choose NVIDIA GPUs for running generative AI applications locally on their PC, which is critical for privacy, latency, and cost-sensitiveAI applications.Professional artists, architects and designers use NVIDIA partner products accelerated with our GPUs and software platform for a range of creative and designuse cases, such as creating visual effects in movies or designing buildings and products.In addition, generative AI is expanding the market for our workstationclass GPUs, as more enterprise customers develop and deploy AI applications with their data on-premises.Headquartered in Santa Clara, California, NVIDIA was incorporated in California in April 1993 and reincorporated in Delaware in April 1998.Our BusinessesWe report our business results in two segments.The Compute & Networking segment is comprised of our Data Center accelerated computing platforms and end-to-end networking platforms including Quantumfor InfiniBand and Spectrum for Ethernet; our NVIDIA DRIVE automated-driving platform and automotive development agreements; Jetson robotics and otherembedded platforms; NVIDIA AI Enterprise and other software; and DGX Cloud software and services.The Graphics segment includes GeForce GPUs for gaming and PCs, the GeForce NOW game streaming service and related infrastructure; Quadro/NVIDIARTX GPUs for enterprise workstation graphics; virtual GPU, or vGPU, software for cloud-based visual and virtual computing; automotive platforms forinfotainment systems; and Omniverse Enterprise software for building and operating metaverse and 3D internet applications.Our MarketsWe specialize in markets where our computing platforms can provide tremendous acceleration for applications.These platforms incorporate processors,interconnects, software, algorithms, systems, and services to deliver unique value.Our platforms address four large markets where our expertise is critical: DataCenter, Gaming, Professional Visualization, and Automotive.Data CenterThe NVIDIA Data Center platform is focused on accelerating the most compute-intensive workloads, such as AI, data analytics, graphics and scientificcomputing, delivering significantly better performance and power efficiency relative to conventional CPU-only approaches.It is deployed in cloud, hyperscale,on-premises and edge data centers.The platform consists of compute and networking offerings typically delivered to customers as systems, subsystems, ormodules, along with software and services.Our compute offerings include supercomputing platforms and servers, bringing together our energy efficient GPUs, DPUs, interconnects, and fully optimized AIand high-performance computing, or HPC, software stacks.In addition, they include NVIDIA AI Enterprise software; our DGX Cloud service; and a growing bodyof acceleration libraries, APIs, SDKs, and domain-specific application frameworks.Our networking offerings include end-to-end platforms for InfiniBand and Ethernet, consisting of network adapters, cables, DPUs, and switch systems, as well asa full software stack.",
backend/scripts/benchmark_results/chunks_injection_from_existing_2026-05-04.json:2521:      "text": "Germany, India, Israel, and Taiwan for fiscal years 2005 through 2023.Note 15 - Shareholders’ EquityCapital Return ProgramIn August 2023, our Board of Directors approved an increase to our share repurchase program of an additional $25.0 billion, without expiration.During fiscalyear 2024, we repurchased 21 million shares of our common stock for $9.7 billion.As of January 28, 2024, we were authorized, subject to certain specifications,to repurchase additional shares of our common stock up to $22.5 billion.From January 29, 2024 through February 16, 2024, we repurchased 2.8 million sharesfor $1.9 billion pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan.Our share repurchase program aims to offset dilution from shares issued to employees.We may pursueadditional share repurchases as we weigh market factors and other investment opportunities.During fiscal years 2024, 2023, and 2022, we paid $395 million, $398 million, and $399 million in cash dividends to our shareholders, respectively.Our cashdividend program and the payment of future cash dividends under that program are subject to our Board of Directors' continuing determination that the dividendprogram and the declaration of dividends thereunder are in the best interests of our shareholders.In fiscal year 2022, we retired our existing 349 million treasury shares.These shares assumed the status of authorized and unissued shares upon retirement.The excess of repurchase price over par value was allocated between additional paid-in capital and retained earnings, resulting in a reduction in additional paidin capital by $20 million and retained earnings by $12.0 billion.Any future repurchased shares will assume the status of authorized and unissued shares.77NVIDIA Corporation and SubsidiariesNotes to the Consolidated Financial Statements(Continued)Note 16 -    213	@pytest.mark.asyncio
   214	async def test_chat_stream_refunds_predebit_when_retrieval_fails(
   215	    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
   216	) -> None:
   217	    session_id = uuid.uuid4()
   218	    document_id = uuid.uuid4()
   219	    user_id = uuid.uuid4()
   220	    ledger_id = uuid.uuid4()
   221	    session_obj = SimpleNamespace(id=session_id, document_id=document_id, collection_id=None, title=None)
   222	    doc_obj = SimpleNamespace(demo_slug=None, custom_instructions=None)
   223	    db = _make_db(
   224	        session_obj,
   225	        doc_obj,
   226	        execute_side_effect=[
   227	            _ScalarOneResult(session_obj),
   228	            _MessagesResult([SimpleNamespace(role="user", content="hello")]),
   229	        ],
   230	    )
   231	    refund_predebit = AsyncMock()
   232	
   233	    monkeypatch.setattr(chat_service_module.credit_service, "get_estimated_cost", lambda _mode: 15)
   234	    monkeypatch.setattr(
   235	        chat_service_module.credit_service,
   236	        "debit_credits",
   237	        AsyncMock(return_value=ledger_id),
   238	    )
   239	    monkeypatch.setattr(
   240	        chat_service_module.corrective_retrieval_service,
   241	        "retrieve_single",
   242	        AsyncMock(side_effect=RuntimeError("qdrant down")),
   243	    )
   244	    monkeypatch.setattr(chat_service_module, "_refund_predebit", refund_predebit)
   245	
   246	    events = [
   247	        event
   248	        async for event in chat_service_module.chat_service.chat_stream(
   249	            session_id=session_id,
   250	            user_message="hello",
   251	            db=db,
   252	            user=SimpleNamespace(id=user_id, plan="pro"),
   253	            mode="balanced",
   254	        )
   255	    ]
   256	
   257	    assert events[-1]["event"] == "error"
   258	    assert events[-1]["data"]["code"] == "RETRIEVAL_ERROR"
   259	    refund_predebit.assert_awaited_once_with(db, user_id, 15, ledger_id)

   423	@pytest.mark.asyncio
   424	async def test_chat_stream_reconciles_predebit_and_records_usage(
   425	    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
   426	) -> None:
   427	    session_id = uuid.uuid4()
   428	    document_id = uuid.uuid4()
   429	    user_id = uuid.uuid4()
   430	    ledger_id = uuid.uuid4()
   431	    chunk_id = uuid.uuid4()
   432	    session_obj = SimpleNamespace(id=session_id, document_id=document_id, collection_id=None, title=None, domain_mode=None)
   433	    doc_obj = SimpleNamespace(id=document_id, demo_slug=None, custom_instructions=None)
   434	    db = _make_db(
   435	        session_obj,
   436	        doc_obj,
   437	        execute_side_effect=[
   438	            _ScalarOneResult(session_obj),
   439	            _MessagesResult([SimpleNamespace(role="user", content="What is MetaX?")]),
   440	        ],
   441	    )
   442	    fake_retrieval = SimpleNamespace(
   443	        retrieved=[
   444	            {
   445	                "chunk_id": chunk_id,
   446	                "document_id": document_id,
   447	                "text": "MetaX 2028 revenue is RMB 7.8 billion.",
   448	                "page": 7,
   449	                "page_end": 7,
   450	                "bboxes": [],
   451	                "section_title": "Valuation",
   452	                "score": 0.91,
   453	            }
   454	        ],
   455	        strategy="semantic_top_k",
   456	        evaluation=None,
   457	        plan=None,
   458	    )
   459	    create = AsyncMock(
   460	        return_value=_FakeStream(
   461	            [
   462	                _FakeChunk("MetaX 2028 revenue is RMB 7.8 billion.[1]"),
   463	                _FakeChunk(
   464	                    None,
   465	                    finish_reason="stop",
   466	                    usage=SimpleNamespace(prompt_tokens=1200, completion_tokens=350),
   467	                ),
   468	            ]
   469	        )
   470	    )
   471	    fake_client = SimpleNamespace(chat=SimpleNamespace(completions=SimpleNamespace(create=create)))
   472	    reconcile_credits = AsyncMock()
   473	    record_usage = AsyncMock()
   474	
   475	    monkeypatch.setattr(
   476	        chat_service_module.action_planner,
   477	        "plan",
   478	        AsyncMock(return_value=SimpleNamespace(uses_rag_answer_path=True)),
   479	    )
   480	    monkeypatch.setattr(
   481	        chat_service_module.query_router,
   482	        "route",
   483	        lambda *_args, **_kwargs: SimpleNamespace(primary_intent=QueryIntent.LOCAL_QA),
   484	    )
   485	    monkeypatch.setattr(chat_service_module.credit_service, "get_estimated_cost", lambda _mode: 15)
   486	    monkeypatch.setattr(
   487	        chat_service_module.credit_service,
   488	        "debit_credits",
   489	        AsyncMock(return_value=ledger_id),
   490	    )
   491	    monkeypatch.setattr(
   492	        chat_service_module.corrective_retrieval_service,
   493	        "retrieve_single",
   494	        AsyncMock(return_value=fake_retrieval),
   495	    )
   496	    monkeypatch.setattr(chat_service_module, "_get_llm_client", lambda _model: fake_client)
   497	    monkeypatch.setattr(chat_service_module.credit_service, "calculate_cost", lambda *_args, **_kwargs: 4)
   498	    monkeypatch.setattr(chat_service_module.credit_service, "reconcile_credits", reconcile_credits)
   499	    monkeypatch.setattr(chat_service_module.credit_service, "record_usage", record_usage)
   500	
   501	    events = [
   502	        event
   503	        async for event in chat_service_module.chat_service.chat_stream(
   504	            session_id=session_id,
   505	            user_message="What is MetaX?",
   506	            db=db,
   507	            user=SimpleNamespace(id=user_id, plan="pro"),
   508	            mode="quick",
   509	        )
   510	    ]
   511	
   512	    assert events[-1]["event"] == "done"
   513	    reconcile_credits.assert_awaited_once_with(db, user_id, ledger_id, 15, 4)
   514	    record_usage.assert_awaited_once()
   515	    assert record_usage.await_args.kwargs["prompt_tokens"] == 1200
   516	    assert record_usage.await_args.kwargs["completion_tokens"] == 350
   517	    assert record_usage.await_args.kwargs["cost_credits"] == 4

   520	@pytest.mark.asyncio
   521	async def test_continue_stream_records_rag_verification_event(
   522	    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
   523	) -> None:
   524	    session_id = uuid.uuid4()
   525	    document_id = uuid.uuid4()
   526	    message_id = uuid.uuid4()
   527	    chunk_id = uuid.uuid4()
   528	    session_obj = SimpleNamespace(id=session_id, document_id=document_id, collection_id=None)
   529	    doc_obj = SimpleNamespace(demo_slug=None, custom_instructions=None)
   530	    original_citation = {
   531	        "ref_index": 1,
   532	        "chunk_id": str(chunk_id),
   533	        "page": 7,
   534	        "page_end": 7,
   535	        "bboxes": [],
   536	        "context_text": "MetaX 2028 revenue is listed as RMB 7.8 billion in the valuation table.",
   537	    }
   538	    assistant_message = SimpleNamespace(
   539	        id=message_id,
   540	        role="assistant",
   541	        session_id=session_id,
   542	        citations=[original_citation],
   543	        content="MetaX 2028 revenue is listed as RMB 7.8 billion in the valuation table.",
   544	        continuation_count=0,
   545	        output_tokens=10,
   546	    )
   547	    chunk = SimpleNamespace(
   548	        id=chunk_id,
   549	        page_start=7,
   550	        page_end=7,
   551	        bboxes=[],
   552	        text="MetaX 2028 revenue is listed as RMB 7.8 billion in the valuation table.",
   553	        section_title="Valuation",
   554	        document_id=document_id,
   555	    )
   556	    db = _make_db(
   557	        session_obj,
   558	        doc_obj,
   559	        assistant_message=assistant_message,
   560	        execute_side_effect=[
   561	            _ScalarOneResult(session_obj),
   562	            _ScalarsResult([chunk]),
   563	            _MessagesResult([assistant_message]),
   564	        ],
   565	    )
   566	    create = AsyncMock(
   567	        return_value=_FakeStream(
   568	            [
   569	                _FakeChunk(" Continued analysis keeps the same MetaX revenue citation.[1]"),
   570	                _FakeChunk(
   571	                    None,
   572	                    finish_reason="stop",
   573	                    usage=SimpleNamespace(prompt_tokens=8, completion_tokens=7),
   574	                ),
   575	            ]
   576	        )
   577	    )
   578	    fake_client = SimpleNamespace(chat=SimpleNamespace(completions=SimpleNamespace(create=create)))
   579	    monkeypatch.setattr(chat_service_module, "_get_llm_client", lambda _model: fake_client)
   580	
   581	    events = [
   582	        event
   583	        async for event in chat_service_module.chat_service.continue_stream(
   584	            session_id=session_id,
   585	            message_id=message_id,
   586	            db=db,
   587	            user=None,
   588	            mode="quick",
   589	        )
   590	    ]
   591	
   592	    assert events[-1]["event"] == "done"
   593	    assert events[-1]["data"]["verification"]["status"] == "pass"
   594	    assert assistant_message.continuation_count == 1
   595	    verification_events = [item for item in db.added if isinstance(item, ProductEvent)]
   596	    assert len(verification_events) == 1
   597	    assert verification_events[0].event_name == "rag_verification_completed"
   598	    assert verification_events[0].metadata_json["retrieval_strategy"] == "continuation"
   599	
   600	
   601	@pytest.mark.asyncio
   602	async def test_continue_stream_reconciles_predebit_and_records_usage(
   603	    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
   604	) -> None:
   605	    session_id = uuid.uuid4()
   606	    document_id = uuid.uuid4()
   607	    message_id = uuid.uuid4()
   608	    user_id = uuid.uuid4()
   609	    ledger_id = uuid.uuid4()
   610	    chunk_id = uuid.uuid4()
   611	    session_obj = SimpleNamespace(id=session_id, document_id=document_id, collection_id=None)
   612	    doc_obj = SimpleNamespace(demo_slug=None, custom_instructions=None)
   613	    original_citation = {
   614	        "ref_index": 1,
   615	        "chunk_id": str(chunk_id),
   616	        "page": 7,
   617	        "page_end": 7,
   618	        "bboxes": [],
   619	        "context_text": "MetaX 2028 revenue is RMB 7.8 billion.",
   620	    }
   621	    assistant_message = SimpleNamespace(
   622	        id=message_id,
   623	        role="assistant",
   624	        session_id=session_id,
   625	        citations=[original_citation],
   626	        content="MetaX 2028 revenue is RMB 7.8 billion.",
   627	        continuation_count=0,
   628	        output_tokens=10,
   629	    )
   630	    chunk = SimpleNamespace(
   631	        id=chunk_id,
   632	        page_start=7,
   633	        page_end=7,
   634	        bboxes=[],
   635	        text="MetaX 2028 revenue is RMB 7.8 billion.",
   636	        section_title="Valuation",
   637	        document_id=document_id,
   638	    )
   639	    db = _make_db(
   640	        session_obj,
   641	        doc_obj,
   642	        assistant_message=assistant_message,
   643	        execute_side_effect=[
   644	            _ScalarOneResult(session_obj),
   645	            _ScalarsResult([chunk]),
   646	            _MessagesResult([assistant_message]),
   647	        ],
   648	    )
   649	    create = AsyncMock(
   650	        return_value=_FakeStream(
   651	            [
   652	                _FakeChunk(" Continued analysis uses the same cited revenue.[1]"),
   653	                _FakeChunk(
   654	                    None,
   655	                    finish_reason="stop",
   656	                    usage=SimpleNamespace(prompt_tokens=800, completion_tokens=160),
   657	                ),
   658	            ]
   659	        )
   660	    )
   661	    fake_client = SimpleNamespace(chat=SimpleNamespace(completions=SimpleNamespace(create=create)))
   662	    reconcile_credits = AsyncMock()
   663	    record_usage = AsyncMock()
   664	
   665	    monkeypatch.setattr(chat_service_module.credit_service, "get_estimated_cost", lambda _mode: 15)
   666	    monkeypatch.setattr(
   667	        chat_service_module.credit_service,
   668	        "debit_credits",
   669	        AsyncMock(return_value=ledger_id),
   670	    )
   671	    monkeypatch.setattr(chat_service_module, "_get_llm_client", lambda _model: fake_client)
   672	    monkeypatch.setattr(chat_service_module.credit_service, "calculate_cost", lambda *_args, **_kwargs: 3)
   673	    monkeypatch.setattr(chat_service_module.credit_service, "reconcile_credits", reconcile_credits)
   674	    monkeypatch.setattr(chat_service_module.credit_service, "record_usage", record_usage)
   675	
   676	    events = [
   677	        event
   678	        async for event in chat_service_module.chat_service.continue_stream(
   679	            session_id=session_id,
   680	            message_id=message_id,
   681	            db=db,
   682	            user=SimpleNamespace(id=user_id, plan="pro"),
   683	            mode="quick",
   684	        )
   685	    ]
   686	
   687	    assert events[-1]["event"] == "done"
   688	    reconcile_credits.assert_awaited_once_with(db, user_id, ledger_id, 15, 3)
   689	    record_usage.assert_awaited_once()
   690	    assert record_usage.await_args.kwargs["message_id"] == message_id
   691	    assert record_usage.await_args.kwargs["prompt_tokens"] == 800
   692	    assert record_usage.await_args.kwargs["completion_tokens"] == 160
   693	    assert record_usage.await_args.kwargs["cost_credits"] == 3

    23	@pytest.mark.asyncio
    24	async def test_empty_vector_results_fall_back_to_lexical(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    25	    document_id = uuid.uuid4()
    26	    lexical_chunk = {
    27	        "chunk_id": uuid.uuid4(),
    28	        "text": "MetaX 2028 revenue appears in this valuation table.",
    29	        "page": 4,
    30	        "page_end": 4,
    31	        "bboxes": [],
    32	        "score": 0.82,
    33	    }
    34	    vector_search = AsyncMock(return_value=[])
    35	    lexical_search = AsyncMock(return_value=[lexical_chunk])
    36	
    37	    monkeypatch.setattr(corrective_module.retrieval_service, "search", vector_search)
    38	    monkeypatch.setattr(corrective_module.retrieval_service, "lexical_search", lexical_search)
    39	
    40	    result = await corrective_module.corrective_retrieval_service.retrieve_single(
    41	        "What is MetaX 2028 revenue?",
    42	        _route(),
    43	        document_id,
    44	        top_k=8,
    45	        db=object(),
    46	    )
    47	
    48	    vector_search.assert_awaited_once()
    49	    lexical_search.assert_awaited_once()
    50	    assert result.strategy == "lexical_correction"
    51	    assert result.retrieved == [lexical_chunk]
    52	    assert result.evaluation.status == "sufficient"

    87	    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
    88	) -> None:
    89	    document_id = uuid.uuid4()

    94	        "page_end": 2,
    95	        "bboxes": [],
    96	        "score": 0.58,
    97	    }
    98	    lexical_chunk = {
    99	        "chunk_id": uuid.uuid4(),
   100	        "text": "The agreement contains a non-compete clause in section 8.",
   101	        "page": 8,
   102	        "page_end": 8,
   103	        "bboxes": [],
   104	        "score": 0.88,
   105	    }

   112	        "Does this agreement contain a non-compete clause?",

   123	        lexical_chunk["chunk_id"],

   128	async def test_table_query_merges_structured_table_evidence_first(
   129	    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
   130	) -> None:
   131	    document_id = uuid.uuid4()

   137	        "page_end": 3,
   138	        "bboxes": [],
   139	        "score": 0.78,

   144	        "text": "Table p.7 #1\n| Company | 2028 Revenue |\n| MetaX | $42m |",
   145	        "page": 7,
   146	        "page_end": 7,
   147	        "bboxes": [],
   148	        "score": 0.96,
   149	        "retrieval_modality": "table",
   150	    }
   151	
   152	    monkeypatch.setattr(corrective_module.retrieval_service, "search", AsyncMock(return_value=[vector_chunk]))

   154	    lexical_search = AsyncMock(return_value=[])
   155	    monkeypatch.setattr(corrective_module.retrieval_service, "table_search", table_search)

   158	    result = await corrective_module.corrective_retrieval_service.retrieve_single(

   167	    lexical_search.assert_awaited_once()

   169	    assert result.strategy == "semantic_top_k+table_evidence+lexical_correction"
   170	    assert result.retrieved[0]["table_id"] == table_evidence["table_id"]
   171	    assert result.retrieved[1]["chunk_id"] == vector_chunk["chunk_id"]

   174	@pytest.mark.asyncio
   175	async def test_planned_single_comparison_adds_subquery_evidence_even_when_vector_is_sufficient(
   176	    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
   177	) -> None:
   178	    document_id = uuid.uuid4()
   179	    initial = {
   180	        "chunk_id": uuid.uuid4(),
   181	        "text": "MetaX and Iluvatar are discussed in the semiconductor report.",
   182	        "page": 1,
   183	        "page_end": 1,
   184	        "bboxes": [],
   185	        "score": 0.91,
   186	    }
   187	    metax = {
   188	        "chunk_id": uuid.uuid4(),
   189	        "text": "MetaX revenue guidance is listed in the valuation section.",
   190	        "page": 6,
   191	        "page_end": 6,
   192	        "bboxes": [],
   193	        "score": 0.84,
   194	    }
   195	    iluvatar = {
   196	        "chunk_id": uuid.uuid4(),
   197	        "text": "Iluvatar target price appears in the comparable companies table.",
   198	        "page": 7,
   199	        "page_end": 7,
   200	        "bboxes": [],
   201	        "score": 0.82,
   202	    }
   203	    search = AsyncMock(side_effect=[[initial], [metax], [iluvatar]])
   204	    lexical_search = AsyncMock(return_value=[])
   205	    monkeypatch.setattr(corrective_module.retrieval_service, "search", search)

   207	    monkeypatch.setattr(corrective_module.retrieval_service, "lexical_search", lexical_search)
   208	
   209	    result = await corrective_module.corrective_retrieval_service.retrieve_single(
   210	        "Compare MetaX and Iluvatar revenue and target price.",

   213	            intents=(QueryIntent.COMPARISON, QueryIntent.TABLE_QUERY),

   225	        initial["chunk_id"],
   226	        metax["chunk_id"],
   227	        iluvatar["chunk_id"],
   228	    }

   231	@pytest.mark.asyncio
   232	async def test_collection_comparison_adds_balanced_per_document_evidence(
   233	    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
   234	) -> None:
   235	    doc_a = uuid.uuid4()
   236	    doc_b = uuid.uuid4()
   237	    initial = {
   238	        "chunk_id": uuid.uuid4(),
   239	        "document_id": doc_a,
   240	        "text": "Report A discusses lower revenue growth.",
   241	        "page": 2,
   242	        "page_end": 2,
   243	        "bboxes": [],
   244	        "score": 0.9,
   245	    }
   246	    balanced_a = {
   247	        "chunk_id": uuid.uuid4(),
   248	        "text": "Report A conclusion favors Cambrian.",
   249	        "page": 8,
   250	        "page_end": 8,
   251	        "bboxes": [],
   252	        "score": 0.82,
   253	    }
   254	    balanced_b = {
   255	        "chunk_id": uuid.uuid4(),
   256	        "text": "Report B conclusion favors Iluvatar.",
   257	        "page": 9,
   258	        "page_end": 9,
   259	        "bboxes": [],
   260	        "score": 0.81,
   261	    }
   262	
   263	    monkeypatch.setattr(corrective_module.retrieval_service, "search_multi", AsyncMock(return_value=[initial]))
   264	    monkeypatch.setattr(corrective_module.retrieval_service, "lexical_search_multi", AsyncMock(return_value=[]))
   265	    search = AsyncMock(side_effect=[[balanced_a], [balanced_b]])
   266	    monkeypatch.setattr(corrective_module.retrieval_service, "search", search)
   267	
   268	    result = await corrective_module.corrective_retrieval_service.retrieve_multi(
   269	        "Compare the conclusions across these reports.",
   270	        _route(
   271	            primary_intent=QueryIntent.COMPARISON,
   272	            intents=(QueryIntent.COMPARISON,),
   273	            scope="collection",
   274	            needs_decomposition=True,
   275	        ),
   276	        [doc_a, doc_b],
   277	        top_k=8,
   278	        db=object(),
   279	    )
   280	
   281	    assert search.await_count == 2
   282	    assert result.plan and result.plan.needs_balanced_coverage
   283	    assert "balanced_compare" in result.strategy
   284	    assert any(item.get("document_id") == doc_b for item in result.retrieved)

     1	from __future__ import annotations

     6	def test_routes_english_whole_document_summary() -> None:

     8

    54	    assert route.primary_intent == QueryIntent.DOCUMENT_SUMMARY
    55	    assert route.coverage == "whole_doc"

    57	
    58	def test_routes_italian_whole_document_summary() -> None:
    59	    route = query_router.route("Riassumi questo documento")
    60	
    61	    assert route.primary_intent == QueryIntent.DOCUMENT_SUMMARY
    62	    assert route.coverage == "whole_doc"
    63	
    64	
    65	def test_routes_arabic_whole_document_summary() -> None:

    67	
    68	    assert route.primary_intent == QueryIntent.DOCUMENT_SUMMARY
    69	    assert route.coverage == "whole_doc"
    70	
    71	
    72	def test_routes_hindi_whole_document_summary() -> None:
    73	    route = query_router.route("इस दस्तावेज़ का सारांश दें")
    74	
    75	    assert route.primary_intent == QueryIntent.DOCUMENT_SUMMARY
    76	    assert route.coverage == "whole_doc"
    77	
    78	
    79	def test_routes_section_summary_separately_from_whole_document() -> None:

    81	
    82	    assert route.primary_intent == QueryIntent.SECTION_SUMMARY
    83	    assert route.coverage == "section"

    85	
    86	def test_routes_table_numeric_query() -> None:
    87	    route = query_router.route("Which company has the highest 2028 revenue in the table?")
    88	
    89	    assert QueryIntent.TABLE_QUERY in route.intents

    92	
    93	def test_routes_financial_metric_question_to_table_query() -> None:
    94	    route = query_router.route("What is the RMB 718 target price based on?")
    95	
    96	    assert route.primary_intent == QueryIntent.TABLE_QUERY

    98	
    99	
   100	def test_plain_page_number_lookup_does_not_become_table_query() -> None:
   101	    route = query_router.route("What is on page 5?")
   102	
   103	    assert QueryIntent.TABLE_QUERY not in route.intents
   104	
   105	
   106	def test_routes_existence_query_as_exhaustive_scan_candidate() -> None:
   107	    route = query_router.route("Does this contract contain a non-compete clause?", domain_mode="legal")
   108	
   109	    assert QueryIntent.EXISTENCE_CHECK in route.intents
   110	    assert route.coverage == "exhaustive_scan"
   111	
   112	
   113	def test_routes_collection_summary_as_decomposition_candidate() -> None:
   114	    route = query_router.route("Summarize these documents and compare their conclusions", is_collection=True)
   115	
   116	    assert QueryIntent.COMPARISON in route.intents
   117	    assert route.needs_decomposition is True
   118	    assert route.scope == "collection"
   119	
   120	
   121	def test_plain_lookup_remains_local_qa() -> None:
   122	    route = query_router.route("What is Cambrian's rating?")
   123	
   124	    assert route.primary_intent == QueryIntent.LOCAL_QA
   125	    assert route.coverage == "top_hits"

frontend/src/i18n/locales/es.json:384:  "landing.faq.a6": "Sí. DocTalk maneja documentos de hasta 500 páginas con fragmentación inteligente y búsqueda semántica.",
frontend/src/i18n/locales/es.json:767:  "about.howItWorks.description": "Detrás de la interfaz, DocTalk combina análisis de documentos, fragmentación, recuperación y generación con modelos de lenguaje para que las respuestas permanezcan conectadas al archivo original. Esa arquitectura es importante porque el producto no intenta reemplazar el documento fuente. Intenta acortar el camino desde la pregunta hasta la evidencia verificable dentro del documento que ya tienes.",
frontend/src/i18n/locales/es.json:1209:  "featuresCitations.layer3Desc": "La última capa es la experiencia de resaltado interactivo. Haz clic en cualquier cita y el visor de documentos se desplaza al pasaje fuente. Para los PDF, utiliza coordenadas de cuadro delimitador para resaltar la región exacta del texto en la página. Para DOCX, PPTX y otros formatos basados en texto, utiliza la coincidencia progresiva de fragmentos de texto para localizar y resaltar el pasaje.",
frontend/src/i18n/locales/es.json:1221:  "featuresCitations.compTextSnippet": "Coincidencia de fragmentos de texto (DOCX/PPTX)",
frontend/src/i18n/locales/es.json:1239:  "featuresCitations.faq2A": "Sí. Cada cita numerada en una respuesta de IA es cliqueable. Al hacer clic, desplaza el visor de documentos al pasaje fuente exacto y lo resalta en amarillo para que pueda leer el texto original en contexto. Para archivos PDF, esto resalta la región del cuadro delimitador en la página. Para otros formatos, utiliza coincidencia de fragmentos de texto.",
frontend/src/i18n/locales/es.json:1241:  "featuresCitations.faq3A": "Sí. El resaltado de citas funciona en los 7 formatos compatibles: PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, TXT, Markdown y URLs. La navegación de citas se adapta a cada formato: PDF utiliza coordenadas del cuadro delimitador, mientras que los formatos basados en texto utilizan coincidencia progresiva de fragmentos de texto.",
frontend/src/i18n/locales/es.json:1814:  "compareHumata.citationsDocTalk": "DocTalk proporciona resaltado de citas en tiempo real. Cada cita en una respuesta de IA es cliqueable: haz clic en ella y el visor de documentos se desplaza al pasaje exacto con una superposición de resalte visual. Para documentos PDF, esto utiliza coordenadas de cuadro delimitador para un resaltado a nivel de píxel. Para otros formatos, utiliza coincidencia de fragmentos de texto. Esta verificación instantánea elimina la necesidad de buscar manualmente las fuentes, ahorrando un tiempo significativo para investigadores y profesionales. Más información sobre",
frontend/src/i18n/locales/pt.json:9:  "about.howItWorks.description": "Por trás da interface, o DocTalk combina análise de documentos, fragmentação, recuperação e geração por modelo de linguagem para que as respostas permaneçam conectadas ao arquivo original. Essa arquitetura importa porque o produto não tenta substituir o documento fonte. Ele tenta encurtar o caminho da pergunta até a evidência verificável dentro do documento que você já possui.",
frontend/src/i18n/locales/pt.json:1576:  "landing.faq.a6": "Sim. O DocTalk lida com documentos de até 500 páginas com fragmentação inteligente e busca semântica, garantindo respostas precisas mesmo em documentos muito longos.",
frontend/src/i18n/locales/zh.json:122:  "feedback.option.source_passage_too_short": "来源片段太短",
frontend/src/i18n/locales/zh.json:1214:  "featuresCitations.layer3Desc": "最后一层是交互式高亮体验。点击任意引用，文档查看器会滚动到源段落。对于 PDF，使用边界框坐标精确高亮页面上的文本区域。对于 DOCX、PPTX 和其他文本格式，使用渐进式文本片段匹配来定位和高亮段落。",
frontend/src/i18n/locales/zh.json:1226:  "featuresCitations.compTextSnippet": "文本片段匹配（DOCX/PPTX）",
frontend/src/i18n/locales/zh.json:1244:  "featuresCitations.faq2A": "是的。AI 答案中的每个编号引用都可点击。点击后文档查看器会滚动到确切的源段落并以黄色高亮显示，让您在上下文中阅读原文。对于 PDF，这会高亮页面上的边界框区域。对于其他格式，使用文本片段匹配。",
frontend/src/i18n/locales/zh.json:1246:  "featuresCitations.faq3A": "是的。引用高亮适用于所有 7 种支持格式：PDF、DOCX、PPTX、XLSX、TXT、Markdown 和 URL。引用导航会适配每种格式——PDF 使用边界框坐标，文本格式使用渐进式文本片段匹配。",
